f: Ran continuously at Maratha Mandir for over 25 years. The train that never left.
c: "Boy meets girl. Father says no. They go to Europe anyway. Entire Punjab gives permission eventually."

f: Highest-grossing Bollywood film of 2001. The reason every NRI cries somewhere over the Atlantic.
c: "Eldest son marries a poor girl. Family is devastated. They fly to London to express this devastation at length."

f: SRK played a terminally ill man who meddles in everyone's relationships. Nobody stopped him.
c: "Best friend is in love with your wife. He is also dying. He decides to help arrange the wedding anyway."

f: Kamal Haasan wrote, directed, and starred. A remake of Mrs Doubtfire but considerably more unhinged.
c: Man dresses as his own child's nanny. Wife does not notice. The wig holds for a startling number of months.

f: Oscar-nominated. Aamir Khan spent a full year actually learning cricket.
c: Village wagers a year of tax on a cricket match against the British Empire. The village has never played cricket. Training begins in sixty days.

f: Flopped in 2004. Now considered SRK's finest. The ultimate sleeper film.
c: "NASA engineer returns to his village. Is horrified. Fixes the water supply. Stays. NASA says nothing."

f: SRK waived his fee for this. Said it was the best script he'd read in years.
c: Women's hockey team is given a coach with a reputation problem. Coach trades the reputation for a clipboard. The clipboard runs the team.

f: A dog named Tuffy had more screen time than half the cast. He earned it.
c: "An entire family attends two weddings across four hours. A dog has a pivotal role in the resolution."

f: Highest-grossing Bollywood film of 2013 at the time. Shot across six states.
c: "Man boards a train to scatter his grandfather's ashes. Accidentally ends up in a Tamil gangster's village. Accidentally gets engaged."

f: Aamir Khan played a 20-year-old at age 44. Convincingly.
c: "Three college idiots. One is secretly a genius. This is discovered ten years later at a goat farm."

f: Based loosely on a true story. The real girl was found and returned to Pakistan in 2015.
c: A pathologically honest man tries to return a lost child across a border that does not want to be crossed. He documents every step. The documents go viral.

f: Karan Johar's directorial debut. Won 8 Filmfare awards. The basketball scene is not realistic.
c: "Man realises he loved his best friend only after she married someone else. His daughter tries to fix this. She is eight."

f: Changed how Indian films showed male friendship. Farhan Akhtar's directorial debut.
c: "Three friends graduate and immediately stop agreeing on anything. One falls for a divorcee twice his age. The others are very judgy about this."

f: The original was shot in 70mm widescreen. Played at one cinema in Mumbai for over five years.
c: "Handicapped man hires two petty criminals to avenge his entire family. The criminals are not prepared for this job."

f: Anees Bazmee directed. Akshay Kumar, Nana Patekar and Anil Kapoor sharing one film is a load-bearing decision the Hindi industry has not repeated.
c: Don's sister wants a respectable groom. Every candidate has a fake ID and a stomach problem. The Mumbai skyline takes the blame.

f: The film sparked real student protests in India. Life imitated art in ways the filmmakers did not expect.
c: "Group of friends make a documentary about freedom fighters and slowly become them. The government does not appreciate this."

f: Aamir Khan directed this. The child actor Darsheel Safary was genuinely dyslexic.
c: Eight-year-old is shipped to boarding school for being difficult. New teacher reads the homework instead of the report card.

f: Kangana Ranaut won the National Award for this. The entire film cost less than most Bollywood item numbers.
c: "Woman gets dumped before her honeymoon. Goes on the honeymoon alone. Returns a completely different person. Ex-fiancé notices."

f: Aamir Khan's highest-grossing film at the time. The nude poster caused significant controversy.
c: "Alien loses his remote control in Rajasthan. Spends the film asking questions about religion that nobody wants to answer."

f: Filmed entirely in Spain. Every extreme sport shown was done by the actors themselves.
c: "Three friends on a road trip across Spain. Two of them love the same woman. Everyone learns something. Mostly about themselves."

f: Karan Johar's most controversial film. Addressed marital unhappiness in Indian cinema for the first time at scale.
c: "Two unhappily married people fall in love. Both spouses find out. Karan Johar explores consequences for once."

f: Shahid Kapoor and Kareena were dating during filming and broke up shortly after. The chemistry was real. So was the breakup.
c: An heir misses his train. A Punjabi woman does not. Hotel bookings proceed out of order.

f: Flopped on release. Now considered the greatest Bollywood comedy ever made. It just needed time.
c: "Two broke men both try to marry the same heiress. Neither has a plan. Both plans fail constantly."

f: Ayan Mukerji's debut feature for Dharma. Konkona Sen Sharma plays a Calcutta transplant trying to write about Bombay while Ranbir Kapoor learns what a kettle is.
c: Rich Bombay boy fails his exams and moves in with an older woman who pays rent. He learns to make eggs. Both find this educational.

f: Aamir Khan gained 25 kg and then lost it all for different phases of filming. True story of the Phogat sisters.
c: "Father drags daughters onto a dirt akhara. Feeds them chicken. They bring back Commonwealth gold. He nods once."

f: Chaiyya Chaiyya was shot on a moving train roof. AR Rahman. One take.
c: "Journalist falls for a woman who is about to blow something up. He knows this. He escalates anyway."

f: Shah Rukh Khan's Om Shanti Om was a tribute to the 70s Bollywood era. 30+ stars appeared in the title song.
c: "Nobody man dies. Comes back with better cheekbones and a film career. Uses both to settle a score."

f: Shot in Chanderi, a real town in MP. Locals were briefed on the film's plot before production began.
c: "Men in a small town are vanishing during the festival. They write a polite note on their doors asking her to come back tomorrow."

f: Imran Khan's debut. A horse played a significant supporting role. Horse's performance was uncontested.
c: "Boy and girl are just friends. Insist on this for two hours. Nobody believes them. They eventually stop believing themselves."

f: Priyadarshan remake of his own Malayalam film. Vidya Balan's performance in the climax is studied in acting schools.
c: "Family moves into a haunted mansion. Calls a psychiatrist instead of a priest. Psychiatrist makes things significantly worse."

f: Zoya Akhtar made the dog the narrator. Aamir Khan voiced the dog. Structural decision of the decade.
c: Industrialist family books a Mediterranean cruise to fix four marriages and one succession plan. The dog narrates the spreadsheet.

f: Irrfan Khan and Nimrat Kaur never share a scene. The film runs entirely on that tension.
c: "Wrong delivery address starts a correspondence between two strangers. They write letters. They never meet. Both are fine with this. Both are not fine with this."

f: Farhan Akhtar lost significant weight for this role. The twist was spoiled in the trailer. Nobody noticed until they watched it.
c: "Man receives motivational calls every morning. Calls are working great. Caller is him."

f: Sriram Raghavan based this on a French short film. The ending deliberately refuses to confirm what really happened.
c: "Man pretends to have a disability. Witnesses something he shouldn't. Cannot reveal what he saw without revealing the lie. Situation worsens weekly."

f: Anurag Kashyap's reimagining of Devdas. The only version where Devdas is sympathetic for about 20 minutes.
c: Punjabi heir loses his fiancee and commits to self-destruction with a budget. Delhi nightlife is happy to invoice.

f: Kangana Ranaut stole the film from a lead who had twice her screen time. Critics could not explain how.
c: "Quiet doctor falls for chaotic woman. Everyone tells him she is wrong for him. They marry. Everyone was correct."

f: Ranbir Kapoor learned to actually play guitar for this role. AR Rahman scored it. The grief is real.
c: Aspiring rockstar is too cheerful to be taken seriously. A break-up is scheduled. The discography improves on contact.

f: Ranbir Kapoor studied Charlie Chaplin for six months. Priyanka Chopra's performance was entirely physical.
c: "Man cannot speak or hear. Still the most charismatic person in every room he enters. Everyone else is embarrassed by this."

f: Farhan Akhtar ran 13km a day for 13 months. His body fat dropped to 4%. Real Milkha Singh cried watching it.
c: "Partition survivor becomes India's fastest man. Loses one race. Never forgives himself. The nation forgave him immediately."

f: Filmed entirely on location in Kolkata during Durga Puja. The city becomes the co-star.
c: "Pregnant woman searches for her missing husband in a city that has never heard of him. She is very determined. The city will regret this."

f: Set in 1953 and loosely based on an O. Henry short story. Sonakshi Sinha watches a tree for roughly forty minutes of screen time.
c: A forger books himself onto a Bengali estate dig as the lead archaeologist. The estate daughter falls for the archaeologist. The dig does not survive winter.

f: Richa Chadha and Vicky Kaushal's breakthrough. Won two prizes at Cannes. Shot on a Rs 4 crore budget.
c: "Two separate grief stories in Varanasi. They never intersect. The Ganga connects them anyway."

f: Imtiaz Ali told the same love story twice across two timelines. One of them learns from the other. Barely.
c: Man celebrates his break-up with a small party. An older man insists on a longer story. The story arrives in a different decade.

f: Hrithik Roshan disguised as the Queen of England in the opening scene. Nobody questioned the casting.
c: "Cop spends the entire film admiring the man he is supposed to arrest. The man is flattered. This is the dynamic."

f: Anushka Sharma produced and starred. The film went to places Bollywood rarely visited. Critics were unsettled.
c: Couple takes the wrong exit off the Gurgaon highway. Witness an honour killing. The village treats it as paperwork that has gone missing.

f: Rishi Kapoor played a 90-year-old. The prosthetics took six hours to apply every day.
c: "Dysfunctional family reunites because grandfather is dying. Every family secret surfaces in order. Grandfather watches."

f: Vishal Bhardwaj set Hamlet in Kashmir during the 1990s conflict. The adaptation is scene-for-scene faithful.
c: "Kashmir. Missing father. Suspicious uncle. Mother who may know more than she says. Shakespeare did not set it here but he could have."

f: John Abraham produced. The film opened a national conversation about sperm donation. Urologist calls were up.
c: "Man is recruited to donate sperm for a fertility clinic. Is very good at this. Wife finds out later. The conversation is difficult."

f: Irrfan Khan won the National Award. The real Paan Singh Tomar held 7 national records. The sports federation lost his paperwork.
c: "National champion athlete becomes a bandit because the sports federation loses his paperwork. The government created this problem."

f: Anushka Sharma's debut. SRK changed his entire body language for the alter-ego Raj. Wife does not notice. This says something.
c: A shy office worker dates his own wife in disguise. The disguise is a haircut. It works for longer than it should.

f: Vikramaditya Motwane's debut. Won best film at multiple festivals. The father character became a reference point for cinematic villainy.
c: "Teenager expelled from boarding school is forced to work in his father's factory. Father is the actual villain of this film."

f: Made on a tiny budget. Became one of the highest-grossing comedies of the year. Proof that chaos scales.
c: "Four idiots learn the location of hidden money. Race every other idiot in India to reach it first. Nobody is qualified for this."

f: Two men faking a relationship for a flat was considered edgy in 2008. The flat was very nice.
c: "Two men tell one lie to rent a flat. The lie works so well it becomes their entire personality for six months."

f: Based on real caste atrocity cases in UP. The director spent a year researching before writing a single scene.
c: "IPS officer posted to rural UP discovers a caste atrocity being covered up. His colleagues are the ones covering it up."

f: Mani Ratnam was attacked at his home after release. AR Rahman's Bollywood debut. Banned in several states.
c: "Hindu-Muslim couple in Bombay. Their city catches fire. A film about love that nobody wanted to make."

f: Ram Gopal Varma. Manoj Bajpayee's breakthrough. Essentially invented the Mumbai crime film as a genre.
c: A penniless migrant lands a job at a Bombay dance bar. He meets a faction lieutenant inside a holding cell. The migrant writes the next chapter.

f: Five and a half hours. Three generations. Anurag Kashyap shot it over 62 days without a single day off.
c: "Three generations of one family spend 70 years trying to kill one other family. Nobody finishes the job. Everyone is also in love with someone inconvenient."

f: Took 6 years to make. Shot in Pune, Maharashtra and Iceland. The goddess is practical effects, not CGI.
c: Man visits a cursed goddess every monsoon to skim from her vault. Treats it as quarterly income. Then takes his son along.

f: Nawazuddin Siddiqui based his character on the real Raman Raghav who terrorised Bombay in the 1960s.
c: Serial killer meets a cop with similar hobbies. They start sharing notes. HR is not running this department.

f: Vishal Bhardwaj's second Shakespeare adaptation. The dialect is from western UP. Ajay Devgn studied it for months.
c: "Othello but in UP gang politics and dialect. A sari replaces the handkerchief. Ends the same way."

f: Ranbir Kapoor gained 10 kg for this role. The film runs 3.5 hours. The ending sets up a sequel nobody agreed to.
c: Heir cannot get a meeting with his father. Begins issuing memos with a chainsaw. The corporate calendar reschedules around the chainsaw.

f: Sanjay Leela Bhansali was attacked on set. The film was banned in four states before release.
c: "King spends an entire war trying to see one woman's face. She has thoughts about this."

f: Anurag Kashyap shelved this film for four years due to political pressure. Piyush Mishra wrote the lyrics while on set.
c: "Law student arrives in Rajasthan and is absorbed into a royalist political movement. Realises too late what he has joined."

f: Anurag Kashyap's most underrated film. Every adult in the frame is failing the child. That is the point.
c: "Child goes missing. Father, ex-husband, stepfather, and the cops all suspect each other. Nobody is looking for the child."

f: Naseeruddin Shah and Anupam Kher. 104 minutes. No filler. Still the most efficient Bollywood thriller made.
c: "Ordinary man calls the police commissioner with a bomb threat. His reasons are not what anyone expects."

f: Vishal Bhardwaj's first Shakespeare adaptation. The witches are corrupt cops who also predict the future. It works.
c: A Shakespeare tragedy relocates to the Mumbai underworld. The witches become corrupt cops with horoscopes. The lieutenant believes them.

f: Sudhir Mishra's most personal film. The Emergency-era politics are accurate. The heartbreak is worse.
c: "Three friends. Emergency-era India. One becomes a revolutionary. One becomes the establishment. One watches both and cannot choose."

f: Nagesh Kukunoor directed. Ayesha Takia and Gul Panag. Shot in Rajasthan and Himachal. Two women who should not meet, must.
c: "Two women. One's husband sentenced to death for allegedly killing the other's. One must travel to get the other to forgive."

f: True story of advocate Shahid Azmi, who was assassinated in 2010. Rajkummar Rao won the National Award.
c: Criminal lawyer defends accused men while the state files the same charge against him. He keeps both files in the same drawer.

f: India's Oscar entry in 2017. A government clerk tries to conduct a fair election in a Naxal-controlled area. Systems resist.
c: "Government clerk is sent to conduct an election in a conflict zone. He insists on doing it properly. Everyone finds this inconvenient."

f: Meghna Gulzar directed. Based on the Aarushi Talwar case. Vishal Bhardwaj wrote the screenplay from public court material.
c: A teenage girl is found dead in a middle-class flat. Three separate agencies reconstruct the same night and file three incompatible versions. Nobody concedes.

f: Anurag Kashyap's most personal film about caste, love, and boxing. The final match sequence runs 15 uncut minutes.
c: Boxer falls for his coach's niece. Coach is also a powerful upper-caste man. The coach controls the federation that schedules the matches.

f: Shakun Batra directed. Shot during the pandemic on long lenses in Alibaug. First Indian direct-to-streaming release from Dharma.
c: A yoga instructor and her cousin's fiance realise their fathers have complicated history. Property paperwork does not help. Nobody books a therapist.

f: Abhishek Chaubey directed. Sushant Singh Rajput. Shot in the Chambal ravines with real dacoits as extras.
c: Dacoits in the Chambal ravines guard a girl the police also want. Honour gets debated. The ravines are unimpressed.

f: Relaunched Mahesh Babu as a mass hero. Remade in four languages. Bhumika Chawla became a star overnight.
c: "Kabaddi champ saves a CM's daughter from a faction rowdy at a Hyderabad festival. Hides her in his Kurnool village for the rest of the film. Her kidnapper brings the village to him."

f: Mahesh Babu reportedly survived on boiled vegetables to get the lean frame. The film's Hindi remake starred Salman Khan three years later.
c: A Hyderabad street operator takes beating after beating and files no complaints. He keeps a mango juice habit and a long list of phone numbers.

f: Mahesh Babu + Trivikram dialogue = instant box office record. The one-liners became memes within hours.
c: "Cop's father is in a 10-year coma. Wakes up to a villain he never caught. Son fakes an alternate reality so dad's heart doesn't give out. Villain is fine with this."

f: Trivikram Srinivas wrote this. Considered by many critics to be Mahesh Babu's finest performance.
c: "Trained assassin takes on the identity of a harmless villager. Has to be gentle. Does not know how. Falls in love anyway."

f: Shot over five years. Highest-grossing Indian film of its time. The waterfall was entirely CGI.
c: "Foundling raised by a waterfall tribe discovers he might be royalty. Climbs the waterfall to confirm. Correct."

f: The answer to #KattapaKyunMara broke the Indian internet. The film made Rs 1800 crore worldwide.
c: "The question everyone asked for two years is finally answered. The answer raises more questions."

f: Shahid Kapoor remade this as Kabir Singh. Both films broke records. Both films started arguments.
c: Brilliant surgeon self-destructs over a break-up. The hospital files complaints. He files more.

f: S.S. Rajamouli. The Naatu Naatu dance sequence won the Oscar. It took three months to choreograph.
c: "Two freedom fighters meet, become best friends, and discover they are on opposite sides. They dance about it."

f: First Telugu film to use motion capture. The horse sequences were entirely practical. The horse was very brave.
c: "Warrior dies 400 years ago mid-love story. Reincarnates as a stuntman. The love story is still unfinished. He notices."

f: Allu Arjun spent 6 months on the accent and mannerisms. The hand tilt became a national gesture.
c: "Illegitimate son of a landlord becomes the most powerful red sandalwood smuggler in Andhra. Tilts his hand. Becomes a meme."

f: Won the National Award for Best Telugu Film. Made on a tiny budget. The food truck was real and operational.
c: "Two people meet for an arranged marriage meeting. Both are trying to escape their families' plans. They accidentally start a food truck."

f: Shot largely in and around Warangal. Sai Pallavi did not have a body double for a single scene.
c: "NRI doctor returns for his brother's wedding in Banswada. Falls for the bride's sister who speaks more Telangana than he can follow. He leaves. She stays put."

f: Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna. The bus scene alone generated 40 million YouTube views.
c: Man kisses a stranger asleep on a bus. Stranger wakes up. They keep ending up at the same wedding. His apologies do not file correctly.

f: Allu Arjun starrer. Butta Bomma became the most streamed Telugu song of the year. Babies swapped is the oldest plot. Still works.
c: "Babies swapped at birth. One grows up rich, one grows up poor. Rich father eventually notices something is off."

f: Siddharth's career-defining role. The father-son dynamic was so accurately written that fathers complained.
c: "Overprotected son rebels against controlling father. Falls for a free-spirited girl. Father is the actual obstacle. Father eventually gets it."

f: Sekhar Kammula's most loved film. No songs during the film. No villain. Just people. Telugu audiences were disoriented then devoted.
c: "Group of engineering students navigate college. No villain. No item numbers. Just life. Telugu audiences were confused then loved it."

f: Highest-grossing Telugu film of 2013. Pawan Kalyan's biggest commercial hit. Trivikram Srinivas dialogue.
c: "Grandson goes undercover in his estranged grandfather's house to reunite the family. Grandfather has no idea. Very large house."

f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The adoption of a village as a CSR concept was modelled on real government schemes.
c: Heir adopts a poor village to prove a point to his father. The village arrives with a list. The list is longer than expected.

f: Mahesh Babu played a Chief Minister who uses an app to receive public complaints. The app trended for a week.
c: "Young man inherits the Chief Minister position. Has no political experience. Governs better than everyone before him. Politicians are annoyed."

f: Jr NTR's transformation from corrupt to principled cop was the most dramatic character arc of that year.
c: Cop takes a bribe from every file on the desk. One file refuses to pay. He spends the rest of the film auditing himself.

f: Pawan Kalyan and Bhoomika Chawla. The hate-to-love formula executed at full Telugu mass-cinema volume.
c: "Two college students argue about love for two hours. He insists marriage is a contract. She insists it isn't. They bet on it. Both lose."

f: Allu Arjun and Trivikram. The comedy sequences between Allu Arjun and Brahmanandam ran longer than the plot.
c: Lazy man gets recruited by both a con artist and the police. Plays both sides. Forgets which side is paying which week.

f: Panja Vaisshnav Tej's debut. The climax on the sea was shot in real waves. Nobody was insured for this.
c: Fisherman's son falls for the landlord's daughter. Landlord disagrees with his whole body. Coastal weather has opinions.

f: Naveen Polishetty and Nazriya Fahadh. The lie they construct across two families escalates geometrically.
c: "Hindu boy and Christian girl want to marry. Both families need elaborate convincing. They construct an increasingly complicated lie."

f: Nani's career-best performance. Based on a true story. Shahid Kapoor remade it in Hindi. Neither version is easy to finish without crying.
c: "Washed-up cricketer attempts a comeback at 36 to buy his son a jersey. Son does not need the jersey. Dad needs this."

f: Venkatesh and Aarthi Agarwal. One of the defining family rom-coms of the early 2000s Telugu scene.
c: "Loud son-in-law arrives at a Rayalaseema wedding. Has an opinion about every function. Falls for the bride's sister. Stays for the next function."

f: Charmi's career-defining performance. The thriller structure was unprecedented in mainstream Telugu cinema at the time.
c: Woman wakes up next to a stranger with no memory of the previous night. She retraces the night in receipts. The receipts get worse.

f: Ravi Teja in a double role. The real cop and the real villager had to be equally convincing. Only one succeeds.
c: "Fierce cop is mistaken for a meek villager and vice versa. Cop has to be meek. Villager has to be fierce. Neither is suited to this."

f: Ravi Teja starrer. The adrenaline addiction concept was written before it was clinically defined in Telugu mass cinema.
c: A man does everything for the adrenaline. He turns a robbery into a charity drive. The villain can never predict the next move.

f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The village deity angle was taken completely seriously. The auto-driver did not want this responsibility.
c: "Auto driver is prophesied to be a god by a village. He does not want to be a god. The village insists. He reluctantly becomes a god."

f: Rana Daggubati's debut. The political idealism felt naïve and then felt necessary. Both at the same time.
c: "IIT-returned son inherits his late CM father's chair at 28. First speech attacks his own party. Second speech attacks his own cabinet. The cabinet votes him out by lunch."

f: Mahesh Babu and Venkatesh together. The family drama ran longer than needed and nobody complained.
c: Two brothers love the same woman. One steps aside. The other does not know for a decade. The reveal arrives at a wedding.

f: Mahesh Babu starrer. Sukumar directed. Visually the most ambitious Telugu film of that decade.
c: Rock star cannot tell what is real. Neither can the audience. The hallucinations have a release schedule.

f: Nikhil Siddhartha and Swathi. Low-budget heist comedy that punched far above its weight.
c: "Small-time Hyderabad thief steals a Ganesh idol. Turns out the idol was carrying a ransom. Everyone in the city wants it back. Including the kidnappers he didn't know existed."

f: Prabhas before Baahubali. The flashback romance is considered one of the finest in Telugu cinema.
c: "Man visits his ancestral village and inherits a decades-old enemy along with the property. Enemy was not listed in the will."

f: Allu Arjun starrer. Trivikram wrote the father character as a specific critique of unchecked generosity.
c: "Son of a generous man inherits his father's generosity and also his father's debts. Must repay both. Somehow does."

f: Remake of the Malayalam original. Vijay Deverakonda in a breakout supporting role. Three love stories, one man.
c: Man falls in love three times across three decades. Each version of him would have hated the previous version.

f: Allu Arjun in a Harish Shankar comedy. The dual-identity gag sustained an entire film. Pooja Hegde co-starred.
c: Catering chef with two LinkedIn profiles. One of them is HR-flagged. The next wedding has both clients.

f: Mahesh Babu starrer. The NRI-returns-and-fixes-farming plot was earnest in a way Telugu blockbusters rarely allow.
c: "Tech billionaire quits his startup the day before IPO to fix one village's irrigation problem. Best friend dies for reasons the board meeting cannot believe."

f: Suhas and Chandini Chowdary. The 1990s period detail was meticulously researched. The ending does not comfort.
c: "Lower-caste boy falls for upper-caste girl in 1990s Andhra. His caste is discovered. The film does not look away from what happens next."

f: Pawan Kalyan and Rana Daggubati. Remake of Ayyappanum Koshiyum. Two men and their ego. That's the film.
c: "SI stops a retired army officer at a checkpoint and confiscates his car. Officer wants an apology. SI wants a confession. The forest between their villages picks a side."

f: Nagarjuna's biggest commercial hit. The anti-marriage argument is presented so convincingly that the reversal has to work harder.
c: Committed bachelor with an argument for every wedding. Meets a woman who keeps a counter-argument. The counter wins by attrition.

f: Siddharth and Trisha. The impossible task structure goes through three iterations. Each one escalates.
c: "Man loves a girl whose family demands an impossible task. He does it. They add another task. He does that too."

f: Prabhas before the mega-stardom. The rowdy-with-a-heart role that built the template.
c: "College rowdy targets the MLA who killed his best friend. Every knife he throws is aimed at the name, not the body. The slum picks a side by the interval."

f: Venkatesh starrer. The criminal family versus romance tension was handled with more nuance than the genre usually allows.
c: "Police informant falls for the sister of the gang he's spying on. Both fathers are crime bosses. Neither father knows about the other. He keeps it that way until one of them is arrested."

f: Allu Arjun and Sheela. The girl's rebellion is treated with more seriousness than the genre usually affords.
c: Girl runs from home repeatedly. A man is dispatched to fetch her each time. He keeps fetching. He stops asking why.

f: Sri Simha and Satya. The crime comedy subgenre in Telugu needed exactly this film.
c: "Two broke men accidentally stumble into a murder investigation while trying to recover their stolen money. They are not detectives. It shows."

f: Venkatesh and Nayanthara. The rom-com formula executed at senior star level. The chemistry was real.
c: An ACP goes undercover to nab a fugitive ex-tax officer. Hides his cop badge from the fugitive's daughter. The cover story keeps acquiring feelings.

f: Three generations of the Akkineni family acting together. Akkineni Nageswara Rao's final film. The meta dimension is overwhelming.
c: Three generations of a family are reincarnations of each other. They all show up at the same address. The address is also reincarnated.

f: Ravi Teja and Richa Gangopadhyay. The hired-troublemaker-falls-in-love plot runs on pure mass energy.
c: "Small-time Hyderabad troublemaker is paid to wreck a family business in Vizag. Falls for the daughter of the man paying him. Decides to wreck his employer instead."

f: Pawan Kalyan starrer. The family history reveal runs through two decades of backstory in one interval.
c: "College student runs over a boy in a late-night hit-and-run. Years later, falls for the boy's sister. She doesn't know. He does. The interval arrives on schedule."

f: Sharwanand starrer. The grandfather-grandson relationship became the benchmark for sentiment in Telugu commercial cinema.
c: Grandfather's last wish is a Sankranti where the whole family shows up. The whole family does not want to show up. The wish is itemised in a letter.

f: Allu Arjun's mass breakthrough. Puri Jagannadh direction. The action sequences were the most expensive in Telugu cinema at that point.
c: "Hyderabad journalist chases a story into a Himachal monastery. Falls for a girl who thinks she's becoming a nun. Her father runs the town he's investigating."

f: NTR Jr and Kajal Aggarwal. Trivikram's dialogue made every line quotable. The hero pretends to be a boyfriend and the pretence becomes real.
c: A man agrees to pose as a girl's fake boyfriend at her sister's wedding. The sister's husband was his old rival. Two pretences collide at a sangeet.

f: Nithiin and Keerthy Suresh. The childhood-enemies-to-lovers arc runs on pure physical comedy.
c: A man and woman are arranged into marriage despite hating each other since childhood. Both plan to sabotage the wedding. Neither plan goes to plan.

f: K Balachander directed. Made Kamal Haasan a star in Telugu. The ending destroyed every audience that saw it.
c: Young couple tries to run further than the families. Geography is not on their side.

f: Priyadarshi and Sree Vishnu. Made on a small budget. Kickstarted a wave of low-budget Telugu comedies.
c: Four engineering friends run a parallel exam-cheating racket. One of them files a real police complaint by accident.

f: K Vishwanath directed. Won the National Award. The classical music sequences are performed live, not playback.
c: "Classical musician takes in a woman shunned by society. Teaches her everything. Society disapproves of this too."

f: Kamal Haasan lost 20 kg for this role. The classical dance sequences are considered the finest in Telugu cinema.
c: "Brilliant classical dancer destroys himself with alcohol. His student surpasses him. He watches this happen from a distance."

f: Kamal Haasan won the National Award. The film is still studied in psychology courses as a depiction of intellectual disability.
c: Intellectually disabled man marries a widow because the village said no. The village then files several complaints.

f: Ram Gopal Varma's directorial debut. Nagarjuna. The chain fight became a reference point for action choreography.
c: "Student takes on a criminal network that controls his college. This is treated as a reasonable response to ragging."

f: Ram Gopal Varma direction. Sridevi and Venkatesh. The on-the-run comedy thriller was unprecedented in Telugu.
c: "Woman accidentally witnesses a crime. Man accidentally gets blamed for a different crime. They run together. Neither trusts the other."

f: RGV's political thriller was shot in real Vijayawada factions' territory. The score by Sri was later lifted into several Hindi masala films.
c: A 1980s Vijayawada faction lord wants justice on his own terms. A journalist takes notes. The notes start running the scoreboard.

f: Ram Gopal Varma direction. Nagarjuna. The whodunit structure was the most complex Telugu thriller of its decade.
c: "Contract killer is hunted by another contract killer. Both keep arriving at the same hotel room. The woman who lives there is the reason."

f: Pawan Kalyan's breakthrough. Won the National Award for Best Telugu Feature in 1998.
c: Boy applies a brute-force algorithm to a no. Repeats annually. The annual review keeps coming back inconclusive.

f: Chiranjeevi starrer. The corruption-fighting narrative ran three hours and felt necessary for every minute.
c: "Mild-mannered college professor runs a vigilante group that assassinates corrupt officials after class. CBI investigator is a former student. Knew it was him. Took the whole film to prove it."

f: Prabhas and SS Rajamouli. The mother-son separation premise is executed at maximum melodrama. It earns it.
c: Mother and son separated during a boat accident. Son grows up on the wrong side of a refugee camp. Decides to commute back.

f: JD Chakravarthy starrer. The film set a template for the Telugu crime-romance hybrid in the mid-90s.
c: "Man finds a girl being sold inside a Hyderabad basti. Breaks her out. The owner is the local corporator. The corporator has a daughter he also loves."

f: Krishna Vamsi directed Nagarjuna and Tabu in this 1996 romance. The soundtrack by MM Keeravani was a top seller that year.
c: Man tries six disguises to win a girl who has said no in three languages. The disguises do not improve. The languages do not change.

f: Anushka Shetty played a queen raised as a prince. The historical accuracy was contested. The performance was not.
c: Queen is raised as a prince to keep the throne. Court has reservations. She runs the kingdom out of the same wardrobe.

f: Adivi Sesh and Regina Cassandra. The interrogation-room structure holds for the entire runtime. The twist recontextualises everything.
c: A man is found dead. A woman confesses. A cop takes her statement. The statement keeps editing itself.

f: Kalyan Ram's career-best. The ancient king in a modern hospital was played entirely straight. No winking at the audience.
c: Ancient king is teleported to the present. Discovers hospitals, democracy, and a closed dynasty. Begins drafting a return ticket.

f: Rana Daggubati and Sai Pallavi. The Naxalite movement backdrop is the most politically serious Telugu film in a decade.
c: Woman falls in love with a Naxalite through his poems. Joins the movement to find him. The poems do not cover field protocol.

f: Nani's most ambitious film. The coal mine landscape is a character. The violence is earned not gratuitous.
c: Coal mining town. Two friends on opposite sides of a faction. The mine has a quarterly violence schedule.

f: Nani starrer. The college-grad-chasing-upper-class-girl template became a small genre after this. Trinadha Rao Nakkina directed.
c: "Engineering grad fake-proposes to a girl to win a bet. Her father recognises him as the boy he thrashed outside a temple. The bet is now his smallest problem."

f: The mera-baap-chor-hai tattoo was Salim-Javed's idea.
c: "Two brothers grow up in a slum. One wears the uniform. One runs the docks. Mother picks a side at dinner."

f: Chandra Barot's gangster caper was written by Salim-Javed and ran into years of production hell.
c: "Smuggler dies. Lookalike street performer is trained to replace him. The diary outlives everyone."

f: Launched the angry-young-man persona. Salim-Javed first offered the lead to Raaj Kumar and Dev Anand.
c: "Orphan cop chases the man with the white horse on his bracelet. Paperwork is not the answer."

f: Took sixteen years to make. One song sequence was shot in Technicolor. Re-released in full colour in 2004.
c: "Prince loves court dancer. Emperor disapproves. Argument settled with a wall and some bricks."

f: Based on R.K. Narayan's novel. Shot simultaneously in Hindi and English with separate directors.
c: "Ex-tour-host runs off with a married dancer. Ends up mistaken for a holy man. Starves for rain to end a drought."

f: Time magazine later listed it among the 100 greatest films of all time.
c: "Unpublished poet is declared dead. Sales soar. He turns up at his own memorial and walks out."

f: India's first film in Cinemascope. Flopped on release. Re-evaluated decades later as a masterpiece.
c: "Filmmaker discovers a girl who can act. Success ruins the marriage. He dies alone in an empty studio chair."

f: Shot on a shoestring. The Mahabharat climax was improvised. Still studied in film schools.
c: "Two photographers document a corpse. The corpse keeps changing costumes. Everyone ends up on stage in an epic."

f: Sagar Ballary's directorial debut, a Hindi remake of the French film Le Diner de Cons. Vinay Pathak became the breakout name.
c: Wealthy executive hosts a dinner where every guest must be an idiot. Tonight's idiot arrives early. The host's evening is itemised in real time.

f: Karan Johar's first non-romance. Released months after SRK was detained at Newark airport.
c: "Man with autism walks across America to tell the president his surname and religion are not the same thing."

f: SRK only got the part after Sanjay Dutt, Ajay Devgn and Aamir Khan had all dropped out.
c: "Young man carves a girl's name into his chest. Phones her on a landline. Calls it love."

f: Farhan Akhtar learned guitar and sang live for the film. The band reunited for a sequel eight years later.
c: "Corporate banker finds his old garage-band mates. They still hate each other. They play one more gig."

f: Imtiaz Ali's most divisive film. Corsica location became a pilgrimage for the film's cult fans.
c: "Boy and girl agree to roleplay on holiday and never exchange real names. Years later one of them cracks."

f: Aniruddha Roy Chowdhury's courtroom drama. The closing monologue became a public-service clip.
c: Three flatmates attend a rock concert. A boy bleeds. A retired lawyer files paperwork the rest of the city avoided.

f: Ashwiny Iyer Tiwari adapted a French novel into a UP town rom-com. Shot on location in Bareilly.
c: "Small-town girl reads an anonymous book about herself. Hunts for the author. Author hires a fake author."

f: Based on the 1987 Opera House heist. Neeraj Pandey shot in period Bombay locations.
c: "Fake tax officials raid jewellers. Nobody realises they are not officials. Real CBI chases the fakes."

f: Sriram Raghavan's revenge noir. Won Varun Dhawan his first serious-actor credit.
c: "Grieving widower lets one bank robber go to prison for life. Then spends a decade hunting the other one."

f: YRF Spy Universe entry. Highest Hindi opener at that point. Shot across seven countries.
c: "Agent goes rogue. Protege is sent to kill him. They chase each other across beaches and Arctic ice."

f: YRF Spy Universe. SRK's comeback after a four-year gap. Biggest opening weekend of his career.
c: "Exiled agent hunts a biological weapon. Fights a former colleague in the skies over Siberia."

f: First film of the YRF Spy Universe. Shot in Istanbul, Havana, and Dublin.
c: "Indian spy in Dublin falls for a Pakistani spy. Both agencies demand a file. They go on the run together."

f: Biopic of Captain Vikram Batra. Went straight to streaming. Shot in actual Kargil terrain.
c: "Young Kargil officer captures two peaks. Calls in coordinates. Dies over a wounded junior. Real story."

f: First mainstream Hindi comedy built on the premise of a live-in relationship.
c: "TV reporter and researcher hide a live-in arrangement from both families. Family announces wedding first."

f: Set in 90s Haridwar. Features a wife-carrying race as the climax. Ayushmann sang his own title track.
c: "Cassette-shop owner is set up with a heavier, better-educated bride. Insults her. Later has to piggyback her."

f: Remake of a Tamil hit. First mainstream Hindi film to centre erectile dysfunction as a plot.
c: "Engaged couple discover a bedroom problem two weeks before the wedding. Both families Google it."

f: Third in the franchise. Shot in Chicago. Biggest Hindi opener of 2013.
c: "Circus owner robs a Chicago bank over and over. Twist turns out to be a second twin nobody mentioned."

f: Karan Malhotra remake. Different climax. Hrithik plays the grown son. Shot largely in Diu doubling for Mandwa.
c: "Boy watches father hang from a tree. Decades later he drives a flashy car into the village to collect."

f: Remake of Mani Ratnam's Tamil Alai Payuthey. Songs by A R Rahman became 2000s wedding staples.
c: "Doctor and architect elope. Two years of chai and fights later she walks out. He chases an ambulance."

f: Imtiaz Ali's Europe-set romance. Commercial flop. Still has a small devoted fandom.
c: "Gujarati tourist loses her engagement ring in Europe. Hires her tour guide to hunt for it city by city."

f: Imtiaz Ali's most austere film. Alia Bhatt won her first major critical notice.
c: "Rich bride-to-be is kidnapped on the eve of her wedding. Refuses to go home when the driver slows down."

f: Rajiv Rai's ensemble hit. Tirchi Topiwale became the wedding procession staple of the 90s.
c: "Three men who hate each other team up to bring down a moustached bandit in a desert hideout."

f: Muzaffar Ali's adaptation of an Urdu novel. Rekha won the National Award for Best Actress.
c: "Little Faizabad girl is kidnapped. Sold to a Lucknow brothel. Grows up writing poetry for rich men."

f: Pankaj Parashar's remake of Seeta Aur Geeta. Sridevi plays twin sisters separated at birth.
c: "Meek heiress and a fearless street girl are switched by accident. Neither family notices for a long time."

f: K Balachander's cross-language romance. One of the earliest films to cast a South Indian hero in a Hindi lead.
c: Tamil boy and a Hindi-belt girl share a wall and no common language. Parents schedule a one-year separation. Both households take notes.

f: Vidhu Vinod Chopra's crime drama. Considered a turning point from 80s masala to 90s Bombay realism.
c: "Brother returns from America. His older brother works for a crime lord. Wedding night ends in a gunfight."

f: Mahesh Bhatt's autobiographical drama. Shabana Azmi won her second National Award for it.
c: "Housewife discovers her filmmaker husband's affair. Leaves. Builds a life alone. Refuses him back at the end."

f: Govind Nihalani police drama. Based on a Marathi story. Considered a watershed for parallel cinema.
c: "Young sub-inspector sees a politician walking free. Beats a suspect to death in frustration. Unravels from there."

f: Balu Mahendra's Hindi remake of his own Tamil film. Ends on one of Hindi cinema's most unanswered shots.
c: "Teacher brings an amnesiac young woman home from a brothel. She recovers. Gets on a train. Forgets him again."

f: Mahesh Bhatt drama. Anupam Kher's debut. He was 28, played a 65-year-old retired school principal.
c: "Retired couple's only son is shot dead in New York. They wait for his ashes. A pregnant tenant moves in."

f: Hrishikesh Mukherjee classic. The Babumoshai line is still quoted. Rajesh Khanna's most enduring performance.
c: A cynical doctor takes in a terminally ill patient. The patient refuses to stop cracking jokes. The doctor starts taking notes.

f: Hrishikesh Mukherjee farce. Remake of a Bengali film. A botany professor impersonates a driver for a prank.
c: "Newlywed professor pretends to be a rustic driver to prank his brother-in-law. Prank lasts two hours."

f: Prakash Mehra entertainer. Features Amitabh's I-can-talk-English monologue shot in a single take.
c: "Village boy joins his employer's hotel as a waiter. Foils a conspiracy against the boss. Monologues in English."

f: Raj N Sippy adaptation of Seven Brides for Seven Brothers. Features Amitabh in a dual role.
c: "Seven unruly brothers live on a farm. Eldest marries a nurse. She tries to civilise the other six."

f: Sai Paranjpye's campus comedy. Shot in South Delhi. Remade in 2013 with a brighter palette.
c: "Two friends keep failing to woo the neighbour. Third shy flatmate accidentally wins her. Both friends sulk."

f: Gulzar drama. Loosely inspired by Indira Gandhi's life. Banned during the Emergency. Re-released after 1977.
c: "Rising politician arrives in a hill town for a campaign. Checks into a hotel run by her long-estranged husband."

f: Gulzar drama. Sharmila Tagore won the National Award for Best Actress for her daughter-and-mother double role.
c: "Doctor returns to a hill town years later. His old flame is dead. Her daughter is now in a brothel."

f: Hrishikesh Mukherjee drama. Score by S D Burman. The closing line is quoted in almost every obituary.
c: "Reclusive alcoholic on an upper floor is pulled out of his shell by a cheerful neighbour with a terminal illness."

f: First Hindi film to cross 100 crore net. Remake of the Tamil original. Aamir built new gym culture.
c: "Industrialist cannot form memories for longer than 15 minutes. Tattoos clues on himself to hunt his girlfriend's killer."

f: Sanjay Leela Bhansali's opulent adaptation. Costliest Hindi film at the time. Nominated for BAFTA.
c: "Law graduate returns from London. Refuses his childhood love under family pressure. Takes up drinking full time."

f: Ashutosh Gowariker's historical. Sparked protests from Rajput groups over its marriage depiction.
c: "Mughal emperor marries a Rajput princess for peace. She sets terms in writing. He actually reads them."

f: Anil Sharma's partition epic. Sold more tickets in 2001 than any other Hindi film that year.
c: "Punjabi truck driver marries a Muslim girl during the partition. Travels to Lahore to bring her back. Uproots a handpump."

f: Farhan Akhtar's remake of the 1978 original. Shot in Malaysia. Song Khaike Paan was reworked for SRK.
c: "Criminal is caught and replaced by a cop informant in Malaysia. Cop who planted him is killed. No one knows he is in."

f: Om Raut's 500 crore Ramayana retelling got roasted for plastic VFX and a chef-viral meme Hanuman dialogue.
c: Exiled prince chases a ten-headed abductor across the ocean. Monkey army assists. Project review is held in public.

f: Trivikram's film was pulled from theatres after a French graphic novel's producers called it an uncredited lift.
c: "Heir fakes being a nobody in his own company to find the traitor. Rides a freight elevator. Sacks everyone who smiles wrong."

f: Puri Jagannadh's pan-India MMA debut cast a real boxer as the champion opponent. The film lost reportedly 100 crore.
c: MMA trainee with a stutter punches his way to Las Vegas. Fights Mike Tyson. The visa application is the smaller mystery.

f: Shot over seven years. The Labrador was trained from a puppy for the role. Audiences cried in theatres nationwide.
c: "Factory worker lives alone and hates it. A Labrador breaks in. He tries to evict her. Road trip happens instead."

f: Rana Daggubati spent months working with real mahouts. The movie was simultaneously released in three languages.
c: "Lone man lives in a forest with elephants. Developers want the forest. He argues with them. The elephants listen."

f: T. Santhosh's Tamil original Kanithan got a faithful Telugu remake. Nikhil played the journalist.
c: "Freelance reporter finds his degree is fake. So is everyone else's in his batch. The racket goes to the top."

f: Mohana Krishna Indraganti adapted Oscar Wilde's Earnest as a Telugu college comedy. Became a sleeper hit.
c: "Two friends invent fake identities to impress two girls. The girls only like the fake names. Everyone has to keep lying forever."

f: Palnati Surya Pratap wrote this with Sukumar. Released on Christmas 2022. Nikhil and Anupama Parameswaran lead.
c: "Man finds a stranger's lost diary and falls in love with the handwriting. Spends the film looking for the girl who wrote it."

f: Sriram Adittya's Telugu buddy-comedy had Nagarjuna and Nani leading. Not a Saratchandra adaptation. Hit in 2018.
c: "Pacifist doctor accidentally shares a hospital stay with a Bombay hitman. They become friends. Goons keep interrupting."

f: Mahesh Narayanan shot the Malayalam thriller on iPhones during lockdown. It dubbed in Telugu on OTT.
c: "Whole film happens on laptop screens and phones. A woman disappears after meeting a guy online. Cousin investigates via video call."

f: Arun Matheswaran's pan-Indian period film. Dhanush leads. Telugu dub ran in AP theatres alongside the Tamil.
c: "British-era soldier quits the army, robs a temple, and becomes a bandit. The village cheers. The British are upset."

f: Karthik Gattamaneni directed. Ravi Teja was cast to look expensive. Pan-India release underperformed.
c: "Cotton farmer in a lonely village is actually a rogue agent. Journalist tries to interview him. Nobody else believes her."

f: Parasuram's follow-up to Sarkaru Vaari Paata. Vijay Deverakonda and Mrunal Thakur lead. Underperformed at the box office.
c: An architect runs his joint family like a project budget. A new lodger upstairs is secretly an heiress writing a thesis on him.

f: Vidyadhar Kagita's debut. Vishwak Sen shot at real Himalayan altitudes. Crowdfunded in parts over four years.
c: Sadhu cannot be touched without dying. Walks to the Himalayas to find a rare herb. Several other plots clock in alongside him.

f: Praveen Sattaru's NIA thriller. Rajasekhar's comeback. Shot across Hyderabad, Portugal, and Georgia.
c: "NIA officer chases a whistleblower across two continents. Villain is a suave IT guy. Everyone runs a lot."

f: Mohan Krishna Indraganti wrote and directed. Nani played a double role. Surabhi and Niveda Thomas headlined separately.
c: "Two women love the same man. One is his fiancee. The other thinks he is dead. He is neither dead nor honest."

f: Krish Jagarlamudi's period film starring Pawan Kalyan. Delayed for years over schedule clashes and re-shoots.
c: "17th-century outlaw steals a diamond from a Mughal emperor to fund a rebellion. Sword fights across Deccan forts."

f: Lokesh Kanagaraj directed. Karthi starred. Telugu dub screened in AP alongside the Tamil. No female lead by design.
c: "Ex-convict picks up his daughter on his release day. Has to drive a truckload of wounded cops through a hostile night first."

f: V.V. Vinayak directed. Venkatesh led as Lakshmi Narayana. Major box-office hit in Andhra.
c: "Orphaned son becomes a tough factory owner. Fights for his adoptive family's honour against a vengeful ex-employee."

f: Ajay Bhupathi's second outing pulled Sharwanand, Siddharth and Aditi Rao Hydari into a three-way coastal drama. Chaitan Bharadwaj scored it.
c: Two childhood friends promise to share everything. A coastal smuggling pull tests the loyalty in instalments.

f: Maruthi directed. Sharwanand and Mehreen Pirzada lead. The entire plot hangs on the hero's cleanliness OCD.
c: "Hero refuses to touch anything dirty. Is engaged to a girl from a village with buffalo. The honeymoon does not go well."

f: Mahesh Babu P. directed. Naveen Polishetty and Anushka Shetty lead. Opened strong on Independence Day weekend.
c: "Standup comic and a chef both decide they are single forever. Then they agree to co-parent a baby. Now they are dating."

f: Chandoo Mondeti directed. Naga Chaitanya's left hand was the entire hook. Based on alien hand syndrome.
c: "Man's left hand has a mind of its own. Belongs to his twin who died in the womb. The hand tries to kill the villain without consulting him."

f: Hemant Madhukar directed. Anushka Shetty and Madhavan lead. Bilingual Telugu-English on Amazon Prime.
c: "Mute painter witnesses her fiance killed in a haunted Seattle house. Cop investigates. Husband may also be a suspect."

f: K. Raghavendra Rao directed. Nagarjuna played Hathiram Baba. Shot on recreated 17th-century Tirumala sets.
c: "Devotee walks barefoot to Tirumala and decides to stay. Falls asleep with his head on the god's foot. The god does not mind."

f: Mani Ratnam's adaptation of the Tamil epic novel. Telugu dub released alongside Tamil. Two-part epic.
c: "Chola prince sails back home to find the empire at war with itself. Everyone is either plotting or in love. Usually both."

f: Mani Ratnam completed the Chola saga in this sequel. Same cast. Telugu dub hit AP screens on day one.
c: "Warring cousins still at it. A dead queen's shadow hangs over every court scene. The throne keeps changing hands."

f: Deva Katta directed. Sharwanand led the Telugu original. Later remade in Hindi with Sanjay Dutt.
c: "Stepson works harder than the real son to inherit the father's political empire. Elections do not wait for families."

f: Damodara directed. Anand Deverakonda played a middle-class groom chasing his runaway new wife.
c: "New groom's wife leaves the house on day three. He does not know if she died, ran away, or just got annoyed. Starts investigating."

f: Radha Krishna Kumar directed. Prabhas and Pooja Hegde lead. Shot partly in Italy with a real train sequence.
c: "Palmist in 1970s Europe predicts deaths for strangers. Meets a woman whose fate line says she will die soon. Panics."

f: Harish Shankar directed. NTR Jr and Samantha lead. The title is a nod to a classic Telugu song hook.
c: "College boy falls for a girl whose brother runs the local faction. Whole family says no. He keeps saying yes until a wedding happens."

f: Satish Vegesna directed. Nithiin and Raashi Khanna lead. Built around a traditional wedding as the climax.
c: "Groom wants a full priest-and-mantra wedding. The bride's modern Hyderabad family refuses. Elders walk out. The wedding happens anyway."

f: Atlee directed. Vijay starred. Telugu dub released in Andhra. Known for a long second-half revenge arc.
c: "Small-town bakery owner used to be a cop. A politician murdered his wife. He pretends to be a baker until the politician visits."

f: Vamsee directed. Ravi Teja led a period bandit biopic. Released on Dussehra 2023. Real Stuartpuram setting.
c: "Real-life village bandit robs only the rich in 1970s Andhra. Police are embarrassed. Villagers are grateful. Ends badly."

f: Shiva Nirvana directed. Nani and Ritu Varma lead. Released direct-to-Amazon Prime during the pandemic.
c: "Youngest brother comes home after years abroad. Family land is being sold behind his back. Elder brother is the problem."

f: Lakshmi Sowjanya directed her debut. Naga Shaurya and Ritu Varma lead. Released Diwali 2021.
c: Heiress wants to pick her own husband. Parents schedule candidates weekly. One candidate refuses to be discharged.

f: A.R. Murugadoss directed. Mahesh Babu led. Bilingual Telugu-Tamil. Built around phone-surveillance-cop trope.
c: Intelligence officer listens to phone calls for distress signals. One caller is the killer. The wiretap audit goes both ways.

f: Yash Chopra's musical won three National Film Awards including Best Popular Film. Karisma Kapoor took home her first major acting prize.
c: Choreographer believes in one true love. Is currently dating two dancers. The rehearsal hall keeps the schedule.

f: J. P. Dutta's war epic is based on the 1971 Battle of Longewala. 'Sandese Aate Hai' became one of the most popular Hindi songs ever.
c: Small army post in the Thar. Pakistani tanks roll in after dark. The post counts the hours until dawn.

f: A. R. Rahman's first original Hindi-film score. Ram Gopal Varma's musical made Urmila Matondkar a leading lady.
c: "Street loafer loves aspiring film heroine. Movie superstar also wants her. She picks the loafer anyway."

f: Karisma Kapoor won the Filmfare Best Actress Award. The film was the year's highest grosser and got a much-discussed kissing scene.
c: "Small-town cabbie drives rich heiress on holiday. Her family disapproves. Cue the rain."

f: David Dhawan's remake of the Tamil film Chinna Mapillai. Govinda later won a Special Jury Screen Award as 'performer of the decade' for it.
c: Railway porter passes himself off as a millionaire's son. Snobby father-in-law approves. Luggage stays in the receipt log.

f: Govinda-Karisma Kapoor pairing. An adaptation of Hrishikesh Mukherjee's 1972 Bawarchi, itself from a 1967 Bengali film.
c: "Rich boy infiltrates girlfriend's joint family as a servant. Cooks for everyone. Wins them over."

f: A loose take on Cyrano de Bergerac. Was 1991's highest-grossing Indian film and sent Kumar Sanu's playback career into the stratosphere.
c: Disabled poet writes love letters. Handsome friend hand-delivers them in person. The handsome friend stops being a friend on schedule.

f: Sooraj Barjatya's follow-up to Hum Aapke Hain Koun. First Bollywood film to be played in Canadian theatres.
c: "Huge joint family. Small misunderstanding. Ramayana parallels invoked at length."

f: David Dhawan remake of the Tamil film Sathi Leelavathi. Sushmita Sen won Best Supporting Actress at the Filmfares.
c: "Model steals husband. Wife and model share advice. Husband ends up begging on the doorstep."

f: David Dhawan remake of the Telugu film Hello Brother, itself inspired by Jackie Chan's Twin Dragons.
c: Twins separated at birth. One a music-college polite. One a Mumbai brawler. Gangsters do not check ID.

f: Akshay Kumar's breakthrough. Directed by Abbas-Mustan, it launched a series of unrelated title-sharing action films.
c: College boys stage a fake kidnap. The ransom shows up real. One body shows up next.

f: Raveena Tandon replaced Divya Bharti after her death during production. 'Tip Tip Barsa Paani' became a cultural monument.
c: Blind millionaire hires a convict to clean up a drug ring. Convict can see fine. The millionaire's reading glasses are on the table.

f: Mahesh Bhatt's debut musical for T-Series. The soundtrack sold 20 million units and remains the best-selling Bollywood album ever.
c: Boy with curly hair meets girl in a raincoat. Both sing into the rain. The cassette label takes notes.

f: Danny Boyle's Mumbai-set drama won eight Oscars including Best Picture. The novel it was based on was by Vikas Swarup.
c: "Chai boy sits on the hot seat. Cops think he cheated. Each answer is a childhood memory."

f: Priyadarshan remake of his own 1984 Malayalam film, which itself borrowed from Dickens's The Strange Gentleman.
c: Tenant assumes the wife is the landlord's daughter. Landlord assumes the tenant is the daughter's fiance. The lease keeps renewing.

f: Rohit Shetty's first Cop Universe film. A remake of the 2010 Tamil film Singam, with Prakash Raj reprising his villain role.
c: Upright cop is transferred to a corrupt taluk. Corrupt MLA underestimates the file. Cars get flipped on schedule.

f: Based on the 1990 Kuwait evacuation of over 170,000 Indians, the largest civil evacuation in history.
c: Kuwait-based businessman stops counting profit and starts counting passports. The phone bill becomes a logistics file.

f: Retells the 1897 Battle of Saragarhi where 21 Sikh soldiers held a post against thousands of Afghan tribesmen.
c: Twenty-one Sikh soldiers. One mud fort. Ten thousand attackers. The fort has one set of arithmetic to the relief column.

f: Rajkumar Hirani's biopic of Sanjay Dutt covers his drug years and his 1993 Bombay blasts arrest. Title is his mother's nickname for him.
c: Film-star son becomes addict, becomes accused, becomes biopic. The biopic has a clear preferred narrator.

f: Biopic of mathematician Anand Kumar, whose Patna coaching program prepares poor students for the IIT entrance exam.
c: "Broke math teacher picks thirty slum kids. Coaches them for the hardest engineering entrance in the world. Rich coaching chain is upset."

f: Shoojit Sircar's Delhi-to-Kolkata road film. Amitabh Bachchan won his fourth National Award for Best Actor for it.
c: "Daughter drives grumpy father across India. Entire plot revolves around his bowel movements."

f: Rohit Shetty's Cop Universe entry. A remake of the 2015 Telugu film Temper. Ajay Devgn cameos as Singham.
c: "Crooked Goa cop does not care. Tragedy strikes a girl he knew. Now he cares a lot. Courts are skipped."

f: Sabbir Khan's action debut for Tiger Shroff. A remake of the 2004 Telugu film Varsham, with Indonesian action-film flourishes.
c: Martial-arts grad has one girlfriend in Bangkok and zero translators. The high-rise is punched through floor by floor.

f: Sridevi's comeback after a 15-year hiatus. The film premiered to a five-minute standing ovation at the Toronto Film Festival.
c: "Housewife is mocked for her broken phrases. Signs up for a Manhattan class. Husband and daughter are in for a lecture."

f: Adapted from Chetan Bhagat's 2009 novel, loosely based on his own marriage. Filmed in IIM Ahmedabad.
c: "Punjabi boy meets Tamil girl at a business school. Parents agree on nothing. Both sides must be won over separately."

f: Saket Chaudhary's comedy grossed over ₹300 crore globally, with most of its earnings from China.
c: Wealthy shopkeeper poses as poor for the school admission quota. Daughter is enrolled. The lifestyle keeps renewing.

f: Ang Lee's adaptation of Yann Martel's novel. Won four Oscars including Best Director. Suraj Sharma's first film role.
c: "Teenager survives a shipwreck. Shares a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger. Both are hungry for 227 days."

f: Adaptation of both 1937 and 1976 versions of A Star is Born. 'Tum Hi Ho' was the year's biggest song.
c: Falling rock star discovers a bar singer with talent. Career trajectories cross. The bar tab keeps the score.

f: Amar Kaushik comedy about premature balding and colourism. Shot in Kanpur and Lucknow.
c: "Door-to-door salesman is going bald. Wears a wig to woo a model. Model wears foundation to look fairer."

f: Inspired by the life of Silk Smitha. Released on the anniversary of her birth. Vidya Balan won the National Award for Best Actress.
c: "Village girl rebrands as a sex-symbol for B-movies. Industry adores her. Industry drops her. The machine keeps rolling."

f: Dhanush's Hindi-film debut. A. R. Rahman's score. Shot mostly in Varanasi.
c: "Boy from Benares stalks neighbour since childhood. She marries someone else. He campaigns for her husband's party instead."

f: Shashanka Ghosh's female-buddy comedy. The year's highest-grossing Hindi film led by women.
c: Four school friends reunite for a mehendi. Each one is mid-resignation from a different relationship. The mehendi runs over schedule.

f: Raj Mehta's directorial debut. Akshay Kumar's highest-grossing India release.
c: "Two couples book the same IVF clinic. Clinic mixes up the sperm samples. Everyone waits for the baby to arrive."

f: Loosely based on Arunachalam Muruganantham, the Tamil entrepreneur who invented low-cost sanitary pad machines.
c: Welder builds a sanitary-pad machine for his wife. Village panchayat files objections. He demos at the UN.

f: Inspired by the true case of Anita Narre, a Madhya Pradesh bride who left her husband over the lack of a household toilet.
c: "Newlywed leaves husband because the house has no latrine. He declares war on open defecation. Plumbing resolves the marriage."

f: Umesh Shukla's satire is based on the Gujarati play Kanji Virudh Kanji, itself inspired by the Australian film The Man Who Sued God.
c: "Earthquake breaks an atheist's shop. Insurance refuses: act of god. He takes god to court. Godmen testify."

f: Gauri Shinde's follow-up to English Vinglish. Shot in Goa and Mumbai across four months in early 2016.
c: "Young cinematographer cannot sleep. Therapist takes her on cycle rides by the sea. Every session is a story about her father."

f: Satish Kaushik's remake of the Tamil film Sethu, based on a real friend of Bala's. The haircut became a cultural phenomenon.
c: "Village thug falls for quiet college girl. She dies. He ends up in an asylum sporting a memorable parting."

f: Subhash Ghai romance pairing Shah Rukh Khan with debutant Mahima Chaudhry. Released for the 50th Independence Day.
c: NRI tycoon imports an Indian bride for his wayward son. The wayward son does wayward. Quiet cousin maintains the guest list.

f: Indra Kumar's Hindi remake of the 1987 Tamil film Enga Chinna Rasa. Was the highest-grossing film of 1992.
c: "Illiterate son trusts stepmother with everything. Wife knows better. Entire household conflict is over property papers."

f: Subhash Ghai musical. First Indian film to crack Variety's top-20 box-office list. A. R. Rahman score.
c: Hill folk singer's daughter is courted by an NRI heir and a city music producer. Both men have an opinion about her career. The music director outranks them.

f: Subhash Ghai crime drama. The 'Choli Ke Peeche' debate reached Parliament. Sanjay Dutt was arrested mid-production.
c: Escaped fugitive falls for a dancer who is undercover. The dancer almost falls back. Both files are open at headquarters.

f: Rajkumar Santoshi courtroom drama. Sunny Deol won a National Award for Best Supporting Actor. Aamir Khan makes a cameo.
c: Homemaker witnesses an assault at a Holi gathering. Family lawyers re-edit the statement. She hires a lawyer the family does not approve.

f: Rajkumar Santoshi's directorial debut. Won seven Filmfare Awards including Best Film. Sunny Deol's breakout.
c: "Amateur boxer's brother is killed by a corrupt industrialist. Courts fail. He breaks out of jail to settle it himself."

f: Ajay Devgn's debut. His entry scene standing on two moving motorcycles has been copied for thirty years.
c: "Gangster's son falls for a nice girl. Dad is not pleased. Son's entry involves balancing on two bikes."

f: Yash Chopra's own favourite of his films. Sridevi plays both mother and daughter. Domestic audiences rejected it at release.
c: Man pines for a woman who dies in childbirth. Years later he is the legal guardian of her daughter. The daughter does not know the file.

f: Vikram Bhatt's remake inspired by On the Waterfront, via a 1988 Vishesh Films debut. Won the Filmfare Best Scene of the Year award.
c: "Street brawler runs errands for a local bully. Sister of an activist changes his mind. Train-track dare is an identity crisis."

f: Rakesh Roshan production. Some scenes shot in Tawang, Arunachal Pradesh. Shah Rukh Khan plays a mute.
c: Coal-mine warlord imports a bride. Bride is meant for him. The mute servant who fetches her is fluent in coal.

f: Rajiv Rai whodunit. Kajol played a villain, unusual for the era. Based on a 1967 French crime novel.
c: Stepson is arrested for killing his politician stepfather. Escapes mid-trial. The visitor's log gets longer.

f: David Dhawan's unofficial Hitch remake. The 'Dus Bahane' song became inescapable.
c: "Mumbai love guru teaches a shy goof how to woo a tycoon's daughter. Guru also starts seeing a widow. Both dates go wrong at once."

f: Ram Gopal Varma's gangster drama. Mohanlal's Hindi debut. Second part of Varma's gangster trilogy after Satya.
c: "Henchman rises in a Mumbai cartel. Boss and protégé fall out over ego and phones. Cartel splits across two continents."

f: Vipul Amrutlal Shah's NRI comedy. Inspired by Manoj Kumar's Purab Aur Paschim, though the director denied it.
c: "Punjabi father flies his Britain-raised daughter home. Arranges a village husband. She agrees to divorce him on arrival."

f: Siddharth Anand drama. Takes its title from a 1977 Rishi Kapoor song. Shot across Switzerland, Italy and Australia.
c: Commitment-phobe collects three wronged exes across three cities. Schedules apologies in receipt order.

f: Farhan Akhtar's war-coming-of-age drama, set against the 1999 Kargil conflict. Was a box-office flop that grew a cult following.
c: "Aimless rich kid enlists in the army to impress a girl. Ends up commanding a mountain assault in Kashmir."

f: Pradeep Sarkar's debut. Adapted from Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay's 1914 Bengali novella. Vidya Balan's Hindi debut.
c: "Childhood friends are secretly married by a private ritual. Fathers scheme. Another rich suitor steps in. Calcutta hums under it."

f: First Indian film shot entirely in Australia. Its script was invited into the Margaret Herrick Library by the Academy.
c: "Couple in Melbourne move in together, no marriage. She gets pregnant. He tries to leave. She builds a crib."

f: Indra Kumar's adult comedy. Spawned three sequels across two decades.
c: "Three bored husbands go out clubbing. Each hooks up with a stranger. The stranger is the same blackmailer."

f: Ram Gopal Varma's unofficial Godfather remake. First of a three-film series spanning over a decade.
c: "Mumbai political godfather grooms his younger son. Elder son resents the pick. One family dinner settles it."

f: Anurag Basu ensemble drama loosely inspired by Billy Wilder's The Apartment. Band Pritam appears performing on-screen.
c: Nine Mumbai stories tangled into nine relationships. Someone is always cheating. The rooftop band keeps a running commentary.

f: Vishal Bhardwaj bought the Kenyan original script for $4,000. The film became the year's critical favourite.
c: "Identical twins with speech quirks. One stammers, one lisps. Both end up holding the same bag of trouble in Mumbai."

f: Sooraj Barjatya's family drama. First Indian film released in cinemas and online simultaneously.
c: "Engagement stretches for half a film. Fire at the bride's house changes the bargain. Groom's family shows up with lotus-purity."

f: Ken Ghosh teen romance. Shahid Kapoor's and Amrita Rao's first leading roles.
c: "College boy dates loud party girl. Quiet best friend waits. Boy figures out his mistake in the last reel."

f: Vikram Bhatt's unofficial What Lies Beneath remake. Shot around The Lawrence School, Lovedale, in Ooty.
c: Estranged couple retreats to a Mussoorie bungalow to repair the marriage. Something else lives upstairs. It has its own appointments calendar.

f: Kunal Deshmukh crime drama produced by Mukesh Bhatt. Launched a short franchise of crime-themed Bhatt productions.
c: "Small-time cricket bookie rises through match-fixing. Girlfriend wants him out. Stadium lights stay on him."

f: Anurag Basu's Unfaithful-inspired thriller. Set and shot in Bangkok. Kicked off a three-film franchise.
c: Bored Bangkok housewife rekindles a college affair. Husband audits the phone bill. The phone bill is itemised.

f: Vidhu Vinod Chopra thriller. Received an R rating in the US for violence. Clashed at Diwali with Mohabbatein.
c: Police officer accidentally orphans a Kashmiri boy. Adopts him. Decades later the boy comes home with a different file.

f: Nagesh Kukunoor's sports drama. Won a National Award. Produced by Subhash Ghai's Mukta Searchlight Films.
c: Deaf-mute village boy wants to bowl for India. Father wants buffaloes. A retired alcoholic insists on the nets.

f: Anurag Kashyap's docudrama on the 1993 Bombay blasts was banned for over two years by a Bombay High Court stay.
c: "Serial-bombs tear through a city in one afternoon. Months of questioning follow. Small-time smugglers become history."

f: Ranveer Singh's film debut. Maneesh Sharma's first directorial. Shot the year Delhi hosted the Commonwealth Games.
c: "Two broke graduates start a wedding-planning firm in Delhi. Business pact says no romance. That pact does not last."

f: Sanjay Leela Bhansali adapted Dostoevsky's White Nights. Marked the debuts of Ranbir Kapoor and Sonam Kapoor.
c: Musician shows up at a blue-lit town. Meets a woman on a bridge. She is waiting for somebody else's appointment.

f: Prakash Jha's village drama set in his native Bihar. One of Madhuri Dixit's most acclaimed performances.
c: Three rural wives object in writing to their feudal in-laws. The panchayat schedules a death penalty. The wives do not file an appeal.

n: Rocket Singh: Salesman of the Year
f: Shimit Amin satire written by Jaideep Sahni. A box-office flop that became a cult favourite for Indian sales teams.
c: "New grad joins an office that sells computers. Boss is a crook. Grad runs a secret honest company from inside the same office."

f: Nishikant Kamat's ensemble drama about the 2006 Mumbai train bombings. Title nods to the old Mohammed Rafi song.
c: Five strangers are on five separate trains. One afternoon explodes several compartments. Each one buys a ticket again the next day.

f: First film to pair Dharmendra with both Deol sons. Uses archival Hindi-film boxing footage as flashback.
c: Disgraced boxer father has two sons. One pro. One doctor. Family meeting reassigns the gloves.

f: Amol Palekar's Rajasthani-folk remake of Mani Kaul's 1973 Duvidha. India's Oscar submission that year.
c: "Husband leaves for a business trip. Ghost takes his face to keep the new bride company. Village ghosts file a complaint."

f: Priyadarshan's remake of Malayalam film Katha Parayumpol, loosely drawing on the Krishna-Sudama legend.
c: "Rural barber is old friends with a visiting movie star. Village demands introductions. Barber never asks."

f: Mani Ratnam's Hindi political drama, simultaneously shot in Tamil as Aayutha Ezhuthu with a different cast. Won six Filmfares.
c: Three Calcutta strangers cross paths on a bridge. A gangster, a student leader, an engineer. One bullet ties their files together.

f: Ram Gopal Varma horror with no songs, unusual for Hindi cinema. Urmila Matondkar won several awards for the performance.
c: "Couple rents a cheap high-rise flat in Mumbai. Wife starts speaking in another voice. Building chairman knows more than he says."

f: Sudhir Mishra drama shot mostly over a single rainy night. Rinke Khanna's last film before retirement.
c: Investment banker's car breaks down on a Mumbai underpass. A sex worker takes shelter in the same lane. Neither logs the conversation.

f: Khalid Mohamed's drama was set against the 1993 Bombay riots. Jaya Bachchan won a Filmfare for Best Supporting Actress.
c: Sister searches six years for a brother lost in riots. Locates him in a militant camp. He has filed paperwork with the wrong office.

f: Prashanth Neel's Kannada gold-mine epic was the most expensive Kannada film ever at the time and launched Yash as a pan-India star.
c: "Bombay mercenary promises his dying mother he'll die rich. Accepts a job killing a gold-mine boss in a Kolar pit. Stays for the job."

f: The sequel hit over 1,200 crore worldwide and made Sanjay Dutt's Adheera the year's most cosplayed Telugu-dub villain.
c: Gold-mine heir keeps the throne by killing an uncle with a Viking beard. Delhi takes a folder out of the cabinet.

f: Rishab Shetty's folk-horror thriller was shot in coastal Karnataka with a real Bhoota Kola performance finale.
c: "Coastal Karnataka buffalo-race champion inherits a land dispute with a forest officer. A village deity settles it at the climax."

f: Rajinikanth's comeback vehicle featured Mohanlal, Shiva Rajkumar, and Jackie Shroff in cameos across three film industries.
c: "Retired prison warden grandpa hunts down an idol-smuggler gang after they grab his cop son. Hums a song. Keeps hunting."

f: V. V. Vinayak's debut and NTR Jr.'s first solo blockbuster. It locked in the Rayalaseema faction template for a decade.
c: "Young man with a haircut comes back to a Rayalaseema village to kill the man who killed his father. Does it over a long interval."

f: Naveen Polishetty co-wrote and starred in this Nellore-set indie that made detective agencies funny again and won him the Nandi Award.
c: Nellore detective with three Yelp reviews finds a body near railway tracks. Decides one low-paying case is worth dying for.

f: Kodi Ramakrishna's horror-fantasy built a full period palace set in Hyderabad and launched Anushka Shetty as a commercial lead.
c: Bride discovers she is the reincarnation of her warrior great-grandmother. An evil uncle is also reincarnated. Palace HR has questions.

f: Sukumar's directorial debut ran 100 days in 150 centres and made Allu Arjun's dyed-blonde hair the decade's most copied college look.
c: "Unhinged college romantic keeps courting a girl who's already engaged. Announces his love at assembly. The friend quietly loses."

f: G. Ashok's bilingual horror was shot simultaneously in Telugu and Tamil. Anushka played a district collector accused of murder.
c: District collector is interrogated in a haunted bungalow. The mansion keeps interrupting. The interrogating officer schedules accordingly.

f: Anil Ravipudi wrote the role for Balakrishna after NBK 107. Sreeleela's role was positioned as an adopted daughter subplot.
c: Convict adopts a girl. Trains her for the army. Files complaints with a politician at every breakfast.

f: Singeetam Srinivasa Rao's high-fantasy classic shot song sequences on imaginary flying rocks and popularised green-screen in Telugu cinema.
c: Tribal-raised prince sails to a floating island to retrieve a princess. Sorcerer protests. The conch is the receipt.

f: Maruthi's sleeper hit made Nani's absent-minded-plant-scientist a meme and was remade in Hindi as Jabariya Jodi.
c: "Botanist with severe short-term memory loss hides it from his Kuchipudi-dancer fiancée. Forgets her name mid-engagement."

f: NTR directed, produced, and played three roles: Karna, Duryodhana and Krishna. The film ran over four hours at release.
c: Mahabharata told from the charioteer's son's view. One actor plays the friend, the hero, and the god giving the hero notes.

f: Boyapati Srinu's hit reset Balakrishna's post-50 career and became 2021's second-biggest Telugu release after Pushpa.
c: Twin brothers: a village protector and a jungle Aghori. The village brother loses a fight. The jungle brother answers the phone.

f: V. Vijayendra Prasad drew loosely on freedom fighter Suddala Hanmanthu and the Razakar movement. The film won six Nandi Awards.
c: "Orphan girl walks to Nehru's Delhi to sing her dead father's freedom songs for the nation. A Hyderabad Nizam landlord tries to stop her."

f: Sujeeth's first pan-India film was shot across UAE, Romania, and Serbia on a 350-crore budget, a Telugu industry record.
c: Two undercover agents chase a 2000-crore heist. The plot files itself in three different cities. The cars get flipped on schedule.

f: Puri Jagannadh's Mahesh Babu film grossed over 70 crore and was based on an original story by Ram Gopal Varma.
c: Young man arrives in Mumbai by train. Decides to replace the top don within thirty days. The blazer holds.

f: Vivek Athreya's vigilante concept came from Malladi's novel about a man who beats up bullies only on Saturdays.
c: "Man only beats up abusers on Saturdays. A sadistic cop finds out. Their shared calendar becomes the problem."

f: EVV Satyanarayana's remake of Twin Dragons was 1994's top Telugu grosser. It was remade in Hindi as Judwaa and Judwaa 2.
c: "Twins separated at birth. One is a rowdy, one is a musician. They swap cities and swap girlfriends without meeting."

f: B. Gopal's Balakrishna actioner was 1992's biggest Telugu grosser and ran 100 days in 30 centres.
c: Inspector slaps corrupt politicians before reading the FIR. Transferred seven times. The slap precedes the file.

n: Naa Peru Surya, Naa Illu India
f: Vakkantham Vamsi's directorial debut. Army-veteran father issues a courtroom-style challenge to his anger-issue soldier son.
