c: "Soldier with anger issues is ordered by a court-martial to fix his temper to rejoin the border unit. Yells through every single fix."

f: Trinadha Rao's film was Ravi Teja's comeback hit after three flops and ran 50 days in Andhra-Telangana despite mixed reviews.
c: "Unemployed man is mistaken for a billionaire heir. Doesn't correct anyone. Marries the heir's fiancée by accident."

f: Parasuram's Mahesh Babu film opened with a 60-crore weekend and framed the plot around a USA-based microfinance lender.
c: US microfinance agent chases an MP's daughter for an unpaid loan. The interest accrues across continents.

f: Rahul Sankrityan's debut horror-comedy was Vijay Deverakonda's first post-Arjun Reddy release and crossed 30 crore.
c: Hyderabad man buys a vintage taxi. The taxi has a previous owner. The previous owner has a route.

f: Srikanth Addala's Telugu remake of Asuran was based on Poomani's novel Vekkai and shot entirely in Anantapur.
c: Farmer watches his son kill an upper-caste landlord. Drags the boy into the forest. The forest is on the syllabus.

f: Chandoo Mondeti based the film on a real 2018 incident where Srikakulam fishermen were captured by Pakistan's maritime forces.
c: "Srikakulam fisherman drifts into Pakistani waters. Spends two years in a Karachi jail. Girlfriend waits by the beach."

f: Sailesh Kolanu's second HIT entry investigated dismembered bodies in Andhra Pradesh and confirmed the HIT films as a shared universe.
c: Homicide cop eats biryani while the corpse is photographed. The case file produces more body parts. He orders extra biryani.

f: Sekhar Kammula's coming-of-age drama used five debutants and featured future stars Vijay Deverakonda and Naveen Polisetty in bit roles.
c: Hyderabad colony kids grow up across one street and one decade. A new family moves in. Friend groups get rezoned.

f: The Telugu-dubbed release of Sudha Kongara's Soorarai Pottru. Inspired by low-cost-carrier founder G. R. Gopinath's memoir.
c: Madurai farmer's son wants to start a budget airline so the village can fly. The Civil Aviation Ministry has many forms.

f: Prasanth Varma's debut anthology used overlapping timelines and tackled child abuse, drug addiction, and queer identity in one film.
c: Hyderabad cafe. Six unconnected stories play across one evening. The receipts begin to overlap.

f: Saagar K Chandra's 90s-set cop thriller went on to become the director's calling card for Virata Parvam and Bangarraju.
c: Encounter specialist chases a cricketer-turned-rowdy through 1990s Hyderabad. Both men buy from the same supplier.

f: K. Viswanath shot dance sequences at Valley of Flowers National Park and the Shanti Stupa. Choreographed by Kelucharan Mohapatra.
c: "Kuchipudi guru's daughter refuses to dance. Hoarding-painter next door sketches her anyway. She keeps finding her portrait on walls."

f: Mohana Krishna Indraganti's meta-romance paired Sudheer Babu with Aditi Rao Hydari and was set in the world of film production.
c: Cartoonist hates film stars. A film star rents the upstairs guesthouse. He draws her in his notebook. The notebook stays open.

f: Karuna Kumar's debut made Film Companion's top 7 Telugu films of 2020 and dramatised caste violence in Srikakulam's cashew belt.
c: Two folk-musician brothers in a Srikakulam cashew town get filed under local caste politics. Years later the file is still active.

f: Rahul Ravindran's directorial debut won the National Film Award for Best Original Screenplay.
c: "Two strangers are introduced at a pre-wedding lunch. They spend the afternoon debating whether to say no. Parents pace outside."

f: Sukumar wrote this with Palnati Surya Pratap directing. The film was banned in some territories for its candid depiction of casual sex.
c: "Catering-service chef falls for an upstairs model. Accepts her past. Then a colony uncle starts gossiping. He stops accepting."

f: Siva Ananth's directorial debut paired ANR with grandson Siddharth and shot song sequences in Innsbruck.
c: "College senior is loved by three women at once. Announces this to his grandfather. Grandfather suggests he pick the quiet one."

f: Merlapaka Gandhi's direct Telugu remake of the Hindi Andhadhun released on Disney+ Hotstar and starred Nithiin as a blind pianist.
c: Pretend-blind pianist witnesses a star-actor's murder. Keeps the act for the police interview. The act starts taking over the diary.

f: Sudhir Mishra's Bombay-noir debut. Plays out across one night in near real time. Opened Rotterdam's Tiger Awards.
c: A man hits a pedestrian with his car at midnight. He phones a local don for help. The don has conditions.

f: Chitrangada Singh's debut. Sudhir Mishra's most quoted film. Took seven years to find funding.
c: Three Delhi college friends pick Naxal activism, diplomatic service, and marrying up. All three choices outlive the seventies, badly.

f: Kareena Kapoor's first National Film Award nomination. Director Anant Balani died mid-shoot; Sudhir Mishra finished the film.
c: A lawyer's car breaks down in the rain. A sex worker takes shelter in the same lane. Neither leaves the conversation they started.

f: Dibakar Banerjee's debut. Anupam Kher took a token fee of eleven rupees. Won the National Award for Best Feature in Hindi.
c: A Delhi accountant buys a plot of land for retirement. A local goon squats on it. The accountant's sons recruit a stage actor.

f: Abhay Deol in the lead. Openly inspired by Roman Polanski's Chinatown. Shot entirely in a fictional Rajasthan town called Lakhot.
c: A Rajasthan irrigation engineer writes detective novels on the side. A woman hires him to tail her husband. The tail leads away from her husband.

f: Rajeev Khandelwal's film debut. Rajkumar Gupta's first feature. Shot on real Mumbai streets without closing any of them.
c: An NRI doctor lands in Bombay and gets a phone call. The call has a destination. The destination has another call.

f: Based on Bunty Chor, a real Delhi serial thief. Dibakar Banerjee's second film. Won the National Award for Best Popular Film.
c: A Delhi boy starts stealing from neighbours. Neighbours become districts. Districts become farmhouses.

f: Abhay Deol pitched the modern Devdas to Anurag Kashyap over dinner. Kalki Koechlin's acting debut.
c: A Punjabi man loses his fiancee and commits to self-destruction. He adds opiates, Delhi nightlife, and a high-school webcam star. The playlist keeps pace.

f: RGV's second film after Siva. Sridevi's only pairing with Venkatesh. Shot mostly on Hyderabad's then-empty outer ring road.
c: A bank clerk accidentally witnesses a robbery. She runs into an unemployed engineer on the street. The robbers follow both, with company.

f: Made simultaneously in Telugu and Hindi (Drohi). RGV's fourth film. Nagarjuna and Urmila's first pairing.
c: A gangster's lover is held in a room by police. The gangster is held in another. They take turns talking their way through the wall.

f: Chandrasekhar Yeleti's debut. Made for under one crore rupees. Won six Nandi Awards including Best First Film.
c: Four broke engineering graduates plan to kidnap a mafia operative for the bounty before he hijacks a flight. Planning takes longer than the kidnap. The mafia notices.

f: Sekhar Kammula's second film. Kamalini Mukherjee's first lead. Recovered its budget on opening weekend, rare for an indie drama.
c: Couple marries above and below their class lines. Both arrive at the honeymoon with separate exit plans. The plans accidentally agree.

f: Sekhar Kammula's follow-up to Anand. Shot on an actual three-day river cruise. The album outsold the film collections.
c: Two arranged-match candidates board the same Godavari cruise with extended families. The river is loud. The families are louder.

f: Sekhar Kammula's biggest commercial hit. Cast assembled from a seven-day Hyderabad audition. Inspired a wave of engineering-college films.
c: Eight engineering students meet on orientation day. They form three couples, one friendship, and one academic backlog. All five hold until graduation.

f: Won Best Feature at the 2008 Nandi Awards. Allari Naresh's switch from comedy sidekick to leading man.
c: A rich brat's fiancee abandons him. He borrows a mechanic's bike and rides toward a pilgrimage. The mechanic has his own agenda.

f: Bhaskar's directorial debut. Siddharth and Genelia reunite after Boys (2003). Remade in Tamil and Hindi within three years.
c: A Hyderabad father plans his son's life in advance, including the fiancee. The son agrees, then meets a different girl. The itinerary does not accommodate.

f: Nagesh Kukunoor self-funded the shoot on seventeen lakh rupees. Widely cited as the film that opened the door for Indian indie cinema.
c: An NRI returns after twelve years and treats his own family like a customer feedback survey. Every aunt files a complaint. The marriage committee convenes anyway.

f: Aparna Sen directed her daughter Konkona Sen Sharma to a National Award in her lead debut. Shot almost entirely on a stalled bus.
c: A Tamil Brahmin mother and a Muslim photographer share a bus seat during a communal curfew. She introduces him to strangers as her husband. The introduction outlasts the journey.

f: Homi Adajania's debut. The entire film was shot in English with a Parsi family at its centre, a first for a mainstream Indian release.
c: A drifter books himself into a dysfunctional Parsi household as an assistant. The household is full of complaints against each other. He takes notes.

f: Milan Luthria directed. The film stitches together three unrelated viewers glued to the same India vs Pakistan World Cup match.
c: A heart patient, an estranged couple, and a call centre employee spend the same afternoon watching the same India versus Pakistan match. None of them are in the same room. All of them should be elsewhere.

f: Madhur Bhandarkar won the National Award for Best Film. Konkona Sen Sharma shadowed real Bombay society reporters for research.
c: Society reporter files party guest lists. Editor heavily redacts the lists. Photos survive every redaction.

f: Tigmanshu Dhulia's debut feature. Irrfan Khan won the Filmfare Best Villain award, which set the template for his early 2000s run.
c: An Allahabad university student union election turns into a regional body count. A meek history student is adopted by the winning faction. The faction expects loyalty in writing.

f: Govind Nihalani adapted Mahasweta Devi's novel. Jaya Bachchan returned to lead acting after a long gap for this role.
c: A Calcutta housewife learns her son is on a morgue list with a case number instead of a name. She starts interviewing his friends like a second mother. Nobody in the family wants the interviews.

f: Rajat Kapoor directed. Ranvir Shorey won the Filmfare Critics award. The film premiered at Cannes Marche before its Indian release.
c: A struggling actor is kidnapped and told to impersonate a mob boss who looks exactly like him. He accepts it as a career upgrade. No exit clause.

f: Samar Khan directed. Loosely inspired by A Few Good Men, relocated to Kashmir. Kay Kay Menon won the IIFA Best Villain trophy.
c: A military lawyer takes a Kashmir court martial assignment as a scheduling favour. The accused refuses to defend himself. Seniors refuse the questions.

f: Gulzar directed and wrote the lyrics. Won the National Award for Best Popular Film. Vishal Bhardwaj's first major score.
c: A Punjab engineering graduate watches his sister's fiance get picked up and returned damaged. Official complaints go nowhere. He stops filing official complaints.

f: Madhur Bhandarkar won the National Award for Best Film on Other Social Issues. Tabu won Best Actress for her role as a Mumbai dance bar performer.
c: A village woman arrives in the city after a family riot and takes the only shift available at a dance bar. Two decades later the shift has outlasted the family. Her son inherits the geography.

f: Chaitanya Tamhane's debut feature. India's official Oscar entry for 2016. Trial scenes shot in long static takes with non-actors.
c: A protest singer is arrested for allegedly provoking a sewer worker's suicide through his lyrics. The trial schedules itself across many months. Everybody goes home for lunch on time.

f: Kanu Behl directed. Produced by Dibakar Banerjee and YRF. Premiered in the Un Certain Regard section at Cannes.
c: Carjacking family schedules a wedding to keep the youngest brother on payroll. The bride brings her own resignation letter. The HR review goes long.

f: Reema Kagti directed. Aamir Khan shot for it between Dhoom 3 prep. The script went through fourteen drafts over three years.
c: A police inspector investigating a late-night car accident keeps getting tips from one specific woman at one specific hotel. His case file expands. His sleep does not.

f: Anand Gandhi's debut. Crowdfunded release through a citizen ticketing drive after every major distributor passed on it.
c: A blind photographer, a monk with liver disease, and a stockbroker with a kidney transplant share one question across three unrelated episodes. The question is whether the original owner still owns anything.

f: Shekhar Kapur directed. Seema Biswas made her feature debut as Phoolan Devi. Released abroad before a contested Indian clearance.
c: A girl from a ravine village is married off, walks out, and ends up running the ravine. Every attempt to file her back into normal life fails. The file keeps reopening.

f: Umesh Bist directed. Sanya Malhotra leads. Shot in Lucknow during the pandemic with a reduced crew.
c: A young widow finishes the funeral snacks and checks her late husband's phone. The phone contains a second inbox. The mourning schedule continues without her.

f: Hansal Mehta directed. Based on the real case of Professor Ramchandra Siras. Manoj Bajpayee won the Asia Pacific Screen Award.
c: A university professor is suspended after a video of his private life is circulated. A young reporter offers to help him reinstate. The university is in no hurry.

f: Subhash Kapoor directed. Loosely inspired by the Sanjeev Nanda BMW hit and run case. Won the National Award for Best Hindi Film.
c: A small-time lawyer with no practice files a public interest petition against a senior counsel who never loses. The senior counsel treats the hearings like a drop-in meeting. The junior files again.

f: Ritesh Batra directed, his follow-up to The Lunchbox. Premiered at Sundance. Shot on location around Mumbai's Gateway of India.
c: A Gateway of India photographer needs to produce a fiancee for his grandmother. A quiet accounting student agrees to hold the pose. The grandmother schedules the next session.

f: Maneesh Sharma directed. Parineeti Chopra and Sushant Singh Rajput lead. Jaipur reportedly saw a bump in wedding-location tourism.
c: Jaipur tour guide gets cold feet at his own wedding. The guest he picks up at the bus stand also has cold feet. Both reschedule.

f: Vinil Mathew directed his debut. Parineeti Chopra plays a scientist with a drug dependency. Dharma and Phantom co-produced.
c: A girl hides in her sister's wedding house after returning from abroad. The groom befriends her like a project manager debugging a process. The wedding schedule continues around them.

f: Nitesh Tiwari directed. Won the National Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi. Shraddha Kapoor and Sushant Singh Rajput lead the ensemble.
c: A middle-aged father tells his hospitalised son about a college hostel competition from decades earlier. The hostel was ranked last. The father narrates the ranking in instalments.

f: Amit Sharma directed. Grossed over two hundred crore worldwide. Neena Gupta and Surekha Sikri both won major awards for this one.
c: A grown son finds out his parents are expecting another child. The office finds out before the extended family does. The extended family has opinions.

f: Laxman Utekar directed. Kriti Sanon won the National Award for Best Actress. Shot in Rajasthan with a small crew during the pandemic.
c: A Rajasthani stage dancer agrees to carry a baby for a foreign couple as a one-time contract. The foreign couple reviews the progress and cancels the order. The dancer is still on schedule.

f: Mudassar Aziz directed. A remake of the 1978 original. Kartik Aaryan leads; Bhumi Pednekar and Ananya Panday round out the triangle.
c: A Kanpur public works officer tells his office colleague his wife is difficult, then tells his wife the colleague is difficult. Both versions end up in the same WhatsApp group. Payroll notices.

f: Anurag Basu directed. Released on Netflix during the pandemic. Pankaj Tripathi anchors the ensemble as a karmic bookkeeper.
c: Four unrelated short stories cross the same corpse. Each story has a different person hiding a different object. The object keeps changing laps.

f: Zoya Akhtar directed. India's official entry for the 2020 Oscars. Inspired by Mumbai rappers Divine and Naezy.
c: A Dharavi college student writes street verses in his notebook and drives cars for a side income. A music producer spots the notebook. The family prefers the side income.

f: Vinil Mathew directed, written by Kanika Dhillon. Released on Netflix. A sequel landed two years later.
c: A small-town wife is questioned about her husband's disappearance and offers a long answer built from pulp novels. The police take notes in real time. The library expands.

f: Shoojit Sircar directed. Varun Dhawan took a pay cut to headline. Shot chronologically over four seasons in Delhi.
c: A hotel management trainee is told a co-trainee has had a serious accident. He starts visiting the hospital off shift. The family politely declines help.

f: Ali Abbas Zafar directed. Second highest opening weekend of 2016. Salman Khan trained in freestyle wrestling for nine months.
c: A Haryana wrestler retires into married life, then loses the married life, then comes back to the mat for a cage league with corporate sponsors. The sponsors want a comeback arc. He supplies one.

f: Sujoy Ghosh directed. Vidya Balan in a double identity. Shot in Chandan Nagar and Kalimpong, both used as full characters.
c: A quiet office worker in a hill town is hit by a car in the morning and put into a coma. The investigating officer opens her diary. The diary contains a second name.

f: Meghna Gulzar directed. Based on Harinder Sikka's novel Calling Sehmat. Alia Bhatt won the Filmfare Best Actress trophy.
c: A Kashmiri student is married into a Pakistani military family as part of a family understanding. The in-laws treat her like a new bride. She treats them like a filing cabinet.

f: Nishikant Kamat directed. Hindi remake of the Malayalam original. Ajay Devgn plays a fourth-standard pass cable operator with a film habit.
c: A cable operator in a Goa village builds a week-long written alibi for his family. He rehearses the alibi against the neighbours first. The neighbours corroborate.

f: Aditya Dhar's debut. Based on the 2016 Indian Army operation across the Line of Control. Grossed over three hundred crore domestic.
c: An army officer requests a desk posting to care for his mother. Within months he is briefed on a cross-border operation and asked to come back to field. The desk file stays open.

f: Jagan Shakti directed. A fictionalised take on India's Mars Orbiter Mission. Highest opening for a non-Khan Hindi film at the time.
c: A senior ISRO scientist is demoted to a small satellite project after a launch fails. She picks a team of mostly dismissed women. Fuel budget is tiny.

f: Atlee directed his Hindi debut. Shah Rukh Khan in a double role. Highest first day opening for a Hindi film at time of release.
c: A prison warden runs a side operation using the inmates as field agents. A senior officer tracks the operation back to a name from decades earlier. The warden shares the name.

f: Venky Kudumula wrote the script with Nithiin in mind and finished it in under six months. It ran Sankranti 2020 before lockdowns hit.
c: An organic farm company needs a managing director. The selection committee picks a vlogger who protests about everything online.

f: Gopichand Malineni shot the film back-to-back with Ravi Teja's comeback arc. Mass songs by Thaman carried the first-week collections.
c: A police officer handles four different troublemakers across four postings. Each one underestimates his filing style.

f: Anil Ravipudi wrote Mahesh Babu as an army officer who rarely wears a uniform. It opened Sankranti 2020 with record numbers.
c: An army man takes leave to visit the family of a fallen colleague. The family is in a political mess and he quietly opens a second file.

f: Anil Ravipudi's Sankranti comedy put Venkatesh and Varun Tej in matching domestic misery. It became Varun's first solo blockbuster.
c: Two married men meet on a flight and compare complaint logs. They form a support group and then make things worse for each other.

f: The sequel tripled the chaos with Sonal Chauhan and Mehreen Pirzada added to the roster. It released in May 2022 after a delayed shoot.
c: Two men hear about an inheritance and convince themselves they are the rightful heirs. Several people file competing claims to the same money.

f: Venkatesh and Naga Chaitanya shared the poster, uncle and nephew on screen and off. KS Ravindra directed.
c: An uncle raises his orphaned nephew and pushes him into a clerical army posting. Then the family gets a ghost problem the HR manual does not cover.

f: Puri Jagannadh's return after a dry patch made Ram Pothineni a Hyderabad slang icon. The film crossed 80 crore on a tight budget.
c: A small-time Hyderabad operator gets a dead cop's memories transferred into his head. He keeps both filing styles active.

f: Venu Sriram remade Pink with Pawan Kalyan after a two-year acting break. It returned him to the box office with Sankranti numbers.
c: Lawyer on sabbatical takes up three women's assault case. Files harder than the prosecution. The room is not prepared.

f: Bharat Kamma's debut paired Vijay Deverakonda with Rashmika Mandanna for their second time. The song Kaadhale picked up a fandom of its own.
c: A student union leader with anger issues falls for a state-level cricketer. She files a complaint of her own and he has to rethink the playbook.

f: Boyapati Sreenu put Allu Arjun in an ex-army avatar with colour-coded villains. It was one of his biggest pre-Pushpa hits.
c: A retired soldier moves back to his village to solve other people's problems. He keeps a strict rule about not hitting a man first.

f: Dil Raju produced Nani's middle-class accountant role. The title stands for Middle Class Abbayi and the songs carried the family audience.
c: A middle-class accountant becomes the household fixer after his brother marries in. He opens a spreadsheet and starts solving relatives.

f: Sukumar wrote it for Jr NTR after researching corporate-revenge plots for months. Most of it was shot in London.
c: A terminally ill father gives his son a single task. The son moves to London and sets up a quiet long-form corporate takedown.

f: Ram Charan played a stunt man for the first time with Srinu Vaitla in the director's chair. Rakul Preet Singh was cast opposite.
c: A stunt double is mistaken for a cop by a game designer with a drug peddler problem. He decides not to correct the misunderstanding. The peddler does not get the memo either.

f: Krishna Chaitanya directed Nithiin with Trivikram producing. It released in April 2018 and had a modest run.
c: A software engineer moves abroad for a job and lands up chasing a stranger he met at a party. The whole thing starts with a lost bag.

f: Sekhar Kammula returned after five years with Naga Chaitanya and Sai Pallavi. The Zumba sequence became the marketing anchor.
c: A Zumba trainer and his small-town partner move from a village to the city. Their families back home file separate objections.

f: Bhaskar paired Akhil Akkineni with Pooja Hegde in a romantic-comedy structured around dating panels. It opened during Dussehra 2021.
c: An NRI groom arrives home ready to marry. A stand-up comedian puts him on a panel and audits his dating history in public.

f: Adivi Sesh wrote and starred in the biopic of Major Sandeep Unnikrishnan, releasing in three languages. Mahesh Babu produced it.
c: A young army officer's career is reconstructed from briefings and home videos. The final assignment takes place inside a Mumbai hotel.

f: Chandoo Mondeti continued his mystery thriller franchise with Nikhil Siddhartha. It picked up Hindi traction through word of mouth.
c: Doctor on vacation chases a missing artifact across two states. A rival researcher pulls the same files. The doctor starts arriving second.

f: Vamshi Paidipally's body-swap action thriller assembled Ram Charan, Allu Arjun and Shruti Haasan. A face transplant plot carried the title.
c: A musician receives a face transplant from a dead stranger. The dead stranger had a pending revenge list.

f: Surender Reddy cast Allu Arjun opposite Shruti Haasan in a brothers-on-opposite-sides setup. S Thaman scored the chartbusters.
c: Two brothers grow up choosing opposite sides of the law. The police officer files reports while the other one cancels them at the door.

f: Harish Shankar reworked a Hindi action hit into Pawan Kalyan's highest opener at that point. The title song held the top spot for months.
c: An irreverent officer transfers to a rural posting and refuses to take anyone seriously. The local strongman tries to file him first.

f: Trivikram wrote it for Allu Arjun after a long gap. The film released during summer 2015 with a family-debt drama structure.
c: A son inherits his late father's debts instead of the estate. He insists on paying each creditor himself and takes a job to prove it.

f: Chandoo Mondeti's debut made Nikhil Siddhartha a thriller lead. The Subrahmanyeswara temple mystery anchored the script.
c: A medical student with a debunking hobby takes a village trip. He lands inside a temple with a closed chamber and starts a research file.

f: Anudeep KV's debut turned three first-time leads into overnight memes. It became the highest-grossing small-budget Telugu comedy of 2021.
c: Three small-town men arrive in the city with no plan and no address. One of them immediately gets booked for a crime that makes no sense.

f: Nandini Reddy remade a Korean hit with Samantha and Lakshmi. The song Nuvvu Nuvvu was a re-recording of an older Telugu classic.
c: A seventy-year-old woman enters a photo studio and walks out looking twenty-four. Her grandson's band suddenly has a new lead singer.

f: Hanu Raghavapudi's debut paired Nani with Mehreen Pirzada in a village gangster-lover setup. It released in February 2016.
c: A small-town romantic falls for the sister of a local faction leader. His plan to impress the family goes through three generations of grudges.

f: Shiva Nirvana's debut cast Nani, Nivetha Thomas and Aadhi Pinisetty in a three-cornered marriage drama. Gopi Sundar scored.
c: A married couple in San Francisco gets a ten-day visitor: her ex-boyfriend, flown in from Hyderabad to win her back. The husband insists on hosting.

f: Anil Ravipudi's 2025 Sankranti release paired Venkatesh with Meenakshi Chaudhary and Aishwarya Rajesh. It crossed 200 crore worldwide.
c: A retired cop takes his wife and his ex-girlfriend under the same roof for the holidays. A kidnapping case arrives by courier.

f: The Sai Kiran Adivi directorial starred Sreeram and picked up a cult following online after its theatrical run.
c: A small-time petty operator is convinced a gold bar is hidden inside a condemned flat. He talks four neighbours into breaking the lease.

f: Vijay Kanakamedala's courtroom drama gave Allari Naresh a career-reset lead role. It dealt with wrongful undertrial detention.
c: A software engineer is arrested on a charge that falls apart on paper. Four years later a lawyer starts filing the countersuit.

f: Sriwass directed Gopichand and Rakul Preet Singh in a wedding-comedy structure. The film released in September 2014.
c: A Hyderabad man crashes a village wedding to stop it. The bride's family has more elders than his own rehearsal list accounted for.

f: Kalyan Krishna Kurasala cast Ravi Teja and Malvika Sharma in a politician-villain setup. Shakthikanth Karthick scored the soundtrack.
c: An orphan with no address sells the only thing he owns at night. A state legislator runs a parallel housing racket and the two lists collide.

f: Vikram Kumar directed Nani in a crime-comedy with five widowed women as a revenge team. It released around Dussehra 2019.
c: A crime novelist agrees to help five widows plan a murder. They treat the meetings like a book club and take minutes.

f: S. S. Raju directed Teja Sajja and Priya Prakash Varrier in a relationship thriller released in 2021. The film debuted on Aha.
c: A young couple on a road trip gets harassed by a stranger who demands papers. What looks like a one-hour problem turns into a night of documentation.

f: Srinivas Avasarala's directorial debut cast Naga Shaurya, Rashi Khanna and Raja Chembolu. It released in June 2014 with indie marketing.
c: A man falls for his best friend's fiancee during the wedding prep. He writes a song about it and hopes the band will stop him.

f: Mallik Ram returned with Siddhu Jonnalagadda and Anupama Parameswaran in the sequel to DJ Tillu. It became a summer 2024 hit.
c: A wedding DJ in Hyderabad agrees to fake a relationship for a one-day favour. A second woman arrives with the same agreement in writing.

f: Chandrasekhar Yeleti directed Gopichand and Taapsee Pannu in a cross-border treasure hunt. It was shot in Ladakh and Hyderabad.
c: A Hyderabad security guard chases his grandfather's diary across the border. His passport has problems and his sense of direction has more.

f: Nandini Reddy's debut with Nani and Nithya Menen used a non-linear structure. Kalyani Malik scored the indie hit.
c: A woman tells her friend how she met her current boyfriend. The story hops through eight years and three mistaken engagements.

f: K. Vijaya Bhaskar's directorial outing launched Aadi Saikumar as a solo romantic lead. Anoop Rubens scored the soundtrack.
c: A college boy falls for a girl whose father is a police officer. He spends the first half winning her and the second half avoiding her address.

f: Venky Atluri wrote and directed Akhil Akkineni in a travel-dating structure. Thaman scored the songs for the January 2019 release.
c: A chronic flirt books a Europe tour and meets a woman who refuses to take him seriously. She keeps a running list of his previous entries.

f: Lakshman Karya directed Sumanth Ashwin and Niharika Konidela in a wedding-reluctant comedy. It released in 2018 after production delays.
c: An engaged couple agrees to do every wedding ritual only because the elders will not stop. Somewhere between the mehendi and the sangeet, they panic.

f: Trivikram directed Jr NTR and Pooja Hegde in a Rayalaseema faction drama. It released in October 2018 with a non-violence arc.
c: A man returns home from London to bury his father. The village faction expects him to pick up the file and continue the ledger.

f: Tharun Bhascker's second film cast four fresh leads on a Hyderabad-to-Goa road trip. Vivek Sagar scored the soundtrack.
c: Four friends in their twenties drive to Goa for a break. Each one is running from a different problem and none of them tells the others.

f: Sashi Kiran Tikka directed Adivi Sesh in a spy thriller co-written by Sesh himself. It ran sleeper-hit theatre legs in August 2018.
c: A junior intelligence recruit is picked for a senior field op on week one. His application paperwork keeps turning up in places it should not.

f: Revathi won the Filmfare Best Actress (Telugu) for this C. Umamaheswara Rao drama, a landmark of Telugu parallel cinema.
c: Hyderabad woman watches a neighbour get arrested for a political murder. Walks downstairs and claims the abandoned infant. Files the paperwork in her own name.

f: Kodi Ramakrishna's devotional hit ran 175 days in Andhra and launched the VFX-heavy goddess-film template for a decade.
c: Village woman bypasses the temple priest and prays to the stone direct. The priest's gang collects on the side. The stone keeps a separate ledger.

f: Jagapathi Babu won his first Nandi Award for Best Actor for this SV Krishna Reddy film, scored on then-new keyboard arrangements.
c: Husband signs the wedding register. His wife's missing first love walks in alive. The lawyer's office reopens at noon.

f: Ram Gopal Varma directed Nagarjuna and Sridevi here, shot largely around the Tirumala hills; Sridevi's only Telugu outing with RGV.
c: A small-time crook is hired to lift the temple crown at Tirumala. A woman on pilgrimage sits next to him for eight hours. He starts answering her questions like a regular pilgrim.

f: Rajiv Menon directed and co-wrote this Telugu-Tamil bilingual produced by Kalaipuli S. Thanu, with Ajith, Tabu, and Aishwarya Rai in one of her earliest leading roles.
c: Two sisters from a wealthy Chettiar family lose the family estate. They move to Chennai with one cookbook and one electric guitar. The city files several invoices.

f: B. Gopal's family drama ran over 100 days across multiple centres. The soundtrack by Koti sold heavily on cassette in 1997.
c: A groom's family shows up with a list of demands the morning of the wedding. The bride's brother hands them a different list. The priest keeps checking his watch.

f: Raghavendra Rao's biopic of the 15th-century composer earned Nagarjuna a National Award and Keeravani a National Award for music direction.
c: Village poet refuses to perform for the local landlord. Walks into the hills to sing for a stone idol instead. The landlord files. The idol does not.

f: Meena starred opposite a child artist in this Muppalaneni Siva drama, remade from the Tamil hit Pasumpon.
c: A young widow takes a teaching job in a village school. The village headman decides she shouldn't have the job. A seven-year-old keeps submitting the same homework.

f: Indraganti Mohana Krishna's debut won the National Award for Best First Film of a Director. Based on a Chalam short story from the 1930s.
c: A village doctor investigates a curse the elders insist is real. The patient list is the village. The cure is filed under heresy.

f: Released during the harvest festival week of 2005, this Muppalaneni Siva film paired Venkatesh with Srikanth in a home-return drama.
c: Dubai NRI lands home for the festival to find his ancestral fields signed away. Brother holds the pen. Builder sends a lawyer instead of cousins.

f: Manjari Makijany directed this little-seen thriller starring Manchu Manoj and Sneha Ullal, edited by Marthand K. Venkatesh.
c: Bangalore engineer wakes up next to a dead stranger. Phones his girlfriend. She asks which hotel. The receipts are gone.

f: Srikanth Addala's directorial debut. Mickey J. Meyer's score was released on tape and CD and became a college-hostel staple for years.
c: Village boy moves to the city for a degree. His classmate's father owns the hostel. The hostel logbook keeps adding home visits.

f: Premji's only Telugu directorial; the film featured mostly newcomers and was shot around the Godavari districts on a small budget.
c: A village girl writes a letter to a boy she saw once at a temple fair. He writes back from Hyderabad. Their parents open both envelopes.

f: Selvaraghavan remade his own Tamil hit for Telugu. The soundtrack by Yuvan Shankar Raja outsold the film itself in cassette markets.
c: College repeat-offender fixates on a balcony across the courtyard. The warden's complaints box fills up. The warden is also his landlord.

f: N. Shankar directed this Sunil action-comedy; the title references a 1967 NTR film but shares no plot elements with it.
c: Florist's son runs errands for the local MLA. Delivers a bouquet to a rival's wedding. The note inside the bouquet is the actual project.

f: A Telugu remake of the Malayalam hit Meesa Madhavan, repackaged for a Hyderabad audience.
c: A village thief romances the landlord's daughter only to recover his own house papers. She believes the romance. The papers do not.

f: Rajendra Prasad won the Nandi Award for Best Actor. Chandra Siddhartha directed this middle-class drama about mortality and family.
c: A newspaper editor spends half his salary on charity for forty years. He dies on a Tuesday. His family discovers the ledger is longer than the will.

f: Director Gangaraju Gunnam's drama; Sharwanand's early lead role, two years before his Gamyam breakout.
c: A 21-year-old who failed third standard works at a rocket-centre canteen. Two strangers convince him to deliver a parcel on Independence Day. The parcel is not lunch.

f: Puri Jagannadh directed; the film was originally planned with a different lead before Nagarjuna stepped in late in pre-production.
c: Hyderabad driver moonlights as a club bouncer. A patron recognises his face from a clipping. The clipping is twenty years older than the bouncer's face.

f: Surya Kiran wrote and directed; dialogues by Sateesh-Ravi. Sumanth's role grew on set as the songwriter premise was tightened.
c: College student joins a student union to woo a classmate. Union president assigns him to lead the Monday protest. The classmate is at the counter-protest.

f: A.R. Murugadoss's Telugu debut as director, shot in Hyderabad and Vishakapatnam with Chiranjeevi in the lead role.
c: Retired army officer starts a chain of good deeds. Each helped person must help three more. The chain hits the wrong office.

f: Nagarjuna's brother Daggubati Venkatesh produced this Konda-directed romance with Tarun and Sridevi Vijaykumar.
c: Boy keeps missing the same girl at the same Hyderabad bookshop. He tries the bookshop cashier as intermediary. The cashier has been reading the letters first.

f: Krish Jagarlamudi's hyperlink drama won the Nandi Award for Best Feature Film. Allu Arjun took a salary cut to play the cable operator role.
c: A cable operator, a rockstar, a sex worker, a Muslim tailor. Same hospital, one night. None of them know each other. The receptionist keeps losing forms.

f: Nag Ashwin's biopic won three National Awards including Best Telugu Feature. Keerthy Suresh trained with Savitri's choreographer's daughter.
c: A Madras film heroine marries a married co-star in secret. The newspapers find out. She keeps showing up to sets anyway until the sets stop calling her.

f: Sankalp Reddy's debut was shot inside a decommissioned submarine set built in Hyderabad and released in three languages.
c: An Indian navy sub crew gets orders to patrol a specific stretch of water during the 1971 war. Nobody is allowed to radio home. Something much louder is already down there.

f: Sukumar built an entire 1980s village set near Hyderabad. Ram Charan learned to operate agricultural pumps for three months before shooting.
c: A partially deaf pump operator in a 1985 village gets his brother elected as the local council president. The outgoing president refuses to leave the office. The ledgers start going missing.

f: Nag Ashwin's directorial debut; Nani shot portions of the film on an actual trek to the Kailash Mansarovar region.
c: A Bangalore investment analyst leaves his promotion interview to scatter a dead friend's ashes in the Himalayas. A free-spirited stranger from a pub insists on coming along. The analyst has never slept in a tent.

f: Director Sree Harsha Konuganti cast mostly newcomers; the reported budget was under five crores, and it became a surprise hit.
c: Four Hyderabad flatmates graduate engineering with no jobs. They pool their savings for a weekend at a beach resort. The resort refuses their debit cards.

f: Priyadarshi played the real-life handloom inventor Chintakindi Mallesham, who patented the Asu weaving machine in 1999.
c: A Pochampally weaver watches his mother do the same arm motion sixteen thousand times a week. He sketches a machine on paper. The engineering colleges he visits send him home with tea.

f: Venkatesh Maha cast entirely non-professional actors from Kancharapalem. Rana Daggubati signed on as producer after a rough cut.
c: A 49-year-old office peon develops feelings for his new boss. A schoolboy develops feelings for a classmate of a different religion. A middle-aged man sells liquor to forget his first wife. All four live on the same street.

f: Munna Kasi's debut was released in March 2020 just days before the pandemic shutdown and later recovered its budget on streaming.
c: Two souls reborn as engineering students wake up inside each other's bodies. The curse breaks if they fall in love in thirty days. Neither one is on schedule.

f: Shiva Nirvana directed this Naga Chaitanya drama with Samantha. The cricket flashback portions were shot at KSCA Bangalore.
c: A Vizag cricketer loses a Ranji selection and spends the next ten years drinking on his father's balcony. His wife keeps a spare key. His father keeps a spare bottle.

f: KVR Mahendra's directorial debut was shot in the Telangana villages of Warangal district, set during the Naxal movement of the 1980s.
c: Landlord's daughter and farmhand's son exchange notes through a courtyard wall. The wall gets rebuilt taller. The notes do not.

f: Dasaradh directed this Prabhas-Kajal Aggarwal romance. Devi Sri Prasad's soundtrack was a top-seller that year.
c: A Hyderabad office manager writes a list of wife qualifications on an Excel sheet. His mother emails the list to a family friend. The family friend's daughter opens the attachment.

f: G. Nageswara Reddy directed this Manchu Vishnu-Raj Tarun buddy comedy set around a wedding complication.
c: Two friends fall for two women at the same wedding. One of them poses as an orphan to win his match over. The other forgets which lie he is currently telling.

f: Ravikanth Perepu's debut was shot on a lean budget in Hyderabad and became a word-of-mouth hit on the multiplex circuit.
c: A US-based banker flies to Hyderabad because his ex-girlfriend says her daughter is missing. The police have no record of the daughter. Neither do the school, the hospital, or the neighbours.

f: Vinod Anantoju's debut was produced by Bhavya Creations; shot entirely in the small town of Kolakaluru in Guntur district.
c: A small-town cook opens a bombay chutney shop in his own village. The bus-stand tea guy already sells chutney. The panchayat meeting gets a new agenda item.

f: Hanu Raghavapudi shot across Kashmir, Srinagar, and London. Dulquer Salmaan learned basic Urdu pronunciation for the letter-writing scenes.
c: A Pakistani law student in London gets a stack of letters addressed to an Indian army lieutenant from the 1960s. She travels to India to deliver them. Every address on the envelopes has been overwritten.

f: Rahul Sankrityan directed; Nani and Sai Pallavi shot the Bengali portions in Kolkata with a local dialogue coach on set.
c: A Hyderabad filmmaker keeps writing scenes about a Bengali priest from the 1960s. His producer thinks he is stealing another writer's work. A Kolkata lawyer flies in with a stack of photographs.

f: Krish adapted the novel by Sannapureddy Venkatarami Reddy. Panja Vaisshnav Tej trained with Rayalaseema shepherds for weeks.
c: Engineering grad fails two interviews and is sent to the hills with the family goats. The hills include tigers, poachers, and zero bars.

f: Venu Yeldandi's debut, set around a Telangana funeral ritual, won two National Awards including Best Telugu Feature Film.
c: A grandfather dies in a village and his three sons gather for the rituals. The priest says one specific bird must eat the ritual rice before the soul passes on. The bird refuses.

f: Sumanth Prabhas's directorial debut was shot in his own village in Nalgonda district with largely non-professional actors.
c: Three jobless village friends decide to start a tent house business. The village calls them a shameless bunch. The bookings come in anyway.

f: Kiran Rao's second feature as director. Written by Biplab Goswami. India's official submission for the 2025 Oscars.
c: Two veiled brides board the wrong trains at a wedding. Their husbands notice late. The railway notices later.

n: Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani
f: Karan Johar's directorial comeback after seven years. Ranveer Singh and Alia Bhatt's first pairing. Shabana Azmi and Dharmendra share scenes.
c: A Punjabi gym boy and a Bengali news anchor want to marry. Both families object for different reasons. A month-long family exchange is scheduled.

f: Vikrant Massey carried the film. Based on Anurag Pathak's biography of IPS officer Manoj Kumar Sharma. Vidhu Vinod Chopra's biggest grosser in a decade.
c: A village boy fails his twelfth-grade exam and decides to sit the civil services anyway. He spends nine years in Delhi photocopy shops. The exam keeps rescheduling him.

f: Vamsi Paidipally's Telugu-Tamil bilingual remake of the French film The Intouchables. Nagarjuna and Karthi headline. Shot simultaneously as Thozha.
c: A quadriplegic industrialist hires an ex-convict as his personal assistant. The convict treats the job like parole. Neither man is invited to the other's functions.

f: Prasanth Varma's superhero film rooted in Ramayana mythology. Made for eight crore rupees, grossed over 350. Launched the Prasanth Varma Cinematic Universe.
c: A small coastal village is protected by a reluctant petty thief who gains strength from a blue stone. The stone has rules. The rules do not include stealth.

f: Nag Ashwin's sci-fi epic. Most expensive Indian film at its release. Shot for Telugu, Hindi, Tamil, Malayalam, and Kannada simultaneously.
c: A bounty hunter in a post-apocalyptic city is chasing a rebel's unborn child. An immortal guardian is chasing the bounty hunter. Several civilisations are running late.
